Along the way, he’s honed his expertise and developed a unique leadership style. For more information, contact Nadia Garcia on \+353 1 572 1159 or email your CV to nadia.garcia@accesshealthcare.ie.

title: Case Pickers - Afternoon Shift
description: **Americold – Helping our customers Feed the World** Here at Americold Dublin we are now recruiting for warehouse operatives. As a Warehouse Case picker you’ll be receiving and dispatching pallets via Warehouse Management System (WMS) using handheld scanners and following all relevant SOPs (Standard Operating Procedures). You'll work in a temperature\-controlled freezer environment for up to 45 minutes per hour, using a manual pallet truck to move safely and position pallets throughout the warehouse. It is vital that all our staff always adhere to the company HACCP plan around food safety, and it will be expected that as a Warehouse Operative, you will familiarize yourself with company standards and procedures. Workplace safety is of paramount importance, and it is your responsibility to adhere to company H\&S policies and best practices. This role involves maintaining the integrity of all products through rigorous quality and processing checks, operating Manual Handling Equipment safely, and efficiently packing cheese products for distribution. **Requirements** ****Essential Criteria**** * Previous experience in warehousing or manufacturing company (essential) * MHE/FLT certificate (Desirable) * Manual handling training (desirable) * Good attention to detail * Ability to work well with others * Patience and the ability to remain calm in stressful situations * Ability to accept criticism and work well under pressure * Flexible and open to change * Excellent verbal communication skills **Working Hours** Sunday \- Thursday (12pm \- 9pm)

title: Check-in Agent
description: **At Sky Handling Partner, our people are our business.** We are delighted to offer an exciting opportunity to join our team as a Check\-in Agent at Dublin Airport. In this customer\-facing role, you will be at the heart of the airport operation, ensuring passengers receive a seamless and positive experience throughout their journey. From check\-in to boarding, you will play a key role in delivering exceptional service while supporting our airline clients in a fast\-paced and dynamic environment. No two days are the same, making this the perfect opportunity for individuals who enjoy working with people and thrive in a busy operational setting. The Role As a Check\-in with Sky Handling Partner, you will work closely with colleagues across multiple departments, Dublin Airport personnel, and airline representatives to ensure the smooth and efficient delivery of passenger services. Strong teamwork and communication are essential, as you will help manage passenger flows, support operational requirements, and respond effectively to any challenges that arise. By working collaboratively and maintaining a customer\-focused approach, you will contribute to a safe, efficient, and enjoyable travel experience for passengers while delivering excellent service to our airline partners. Key Responsibilities* Process passengers and their baggage for the correct flight in accordance with established procedures. * Verify passenger documentation in line with immigration, security, and airline requirements. * Ensure all required passenger security questions are asked in accordance with IAA directives. * Assist disrupted passengers in line with airline procedures and the instructions of Supervisors and Duty Managers. * Provide assistance to passengers who require special assistance or additional support. * Manage the boarding process accurately, ensuring passenger numbers are correctly recorded and all safety and security procedures are followed. * Deliver exceptional customer service in a professional, positive, and friendly manner at all times. * Meet arriving passengers and direct or escort them through the appropriate immigration channels. * Maintain the highest standards of grooming and presentation in accordance with company policy. * Report any system errors, procedural issues, or operational concerns to your Supervisor. * Exercise particular care to prevent duplicate check\-in errors. * Undertake additional duties as required, including providing support to other operational departments. Person Specification* Previous customer service experience. * Ability to work effectively under pressure in a fast\-paced environment. * Strong organisational, communication, and interpersonal skills. * Flexibility to work rostered shifts and adapt to operational disruptions when required. * Professional attitude with the ability to follow instructions from Duty Managers and the Station Manager. * Excellent attendance, punctuality, and timekeeping record. **Requirements** * + **Minimum Age Requirement – 18 Years:** Applicants must be at least 18 years of age due to the nature of the role and operational requirements within the airport environment. + **Successful Completion of a 5\-Year Security Background Check and Garda Vetting:** All successful candidates must pass a comprehensive 5\-year background check and Garda Vetting process in line with aviation security regulations. + **Right to Live and Work in the European Union:** Candidates must have the legal right to live and work within the European Union. + **Excellent Written and Verbal English:** Strong communication skills are essential, including the ability to understand instructions, communicate effectively with passengers and colleagues, and complete documentation accurately. + **Flexibility to Work Rostered Shifts:** This role operates on a shift basis and includes early mornings, late evenings, weekends, and public holidays. Flexibility is essential to support operational requirements and flight disruptions. Please note that shifts will primarily commence from 03:00\. + **Ability to Work Under Pressure:** The airport environment is fast\-paced and dynamic. Candidates must be capable of remaining calm, professional, and organised while working in time\-sensitive situations. + **Excellent Attendance and Timekeeping:** Reliability and punctuality are critical to maintaining operational efficiency and delivering a high standard of service to our airline clients and passengers. + **Commitment to SHP Grooming and Uniform Standards:** Candidates must maintain a professional appearance and adhere to all company grooming and uniform requirements. * Desirable Criteria * + Manual Handling Certificate. + Previous experience working within an operational environment. + Previous customer service experience. * Contract and Hours * Join a dedicated and collaborative team at Sky Handling Partner, where your commitment to safety, teamwork, and customer service will be recognised and rewarded. * This is a permanent position with a six\-month probationary period. The role offers a competitive hourly rate of **€14\.79 – €14\.94 (inclusive)**, with overtime paid at **time and a half**. * The contract guarantees **20 hours per week**; however, additional hours are regularly available based on operational requirements. Flexibility is essential, as aircraft delays, schedule changes, and passenger demand may require additional working hours. * Shift lengths typically range from **4 to 10 hours** and include early starts, late finishes, weekends, and public holidays, providing a varied and dynamic working environment. **Benefits** At Sky Handling Partner, we are committed to supporting the wellbeing, development, and success of our employees. We offer a range of benefits designed to support both your professional growth and personal wellbeing, including: * **Career Development Opportunities** – Ongoing training and opportunities for progression within the aviation industry. * **Pension Scheme** – Helping you plan and prepare for your future. * **Employee Assistance Programme (EAP)** – Access to 24/7 confidential support and wellbeing services. * **Sick Pay Scheme** – Providing additional support when you need it most. * **Bike to Work Scheme** – Encouraging healthier and more sustainable commuting options. * **Long Service Awards** – Recognising and rewarding commitment and dedication to the business. * **Discounted Insurance Policies** – Exclusive employee discounts through Marsh Insurance Brokers Ireland. * **Reduced\-Rate Employee Parking** – Convenient on\-site parking available at a discounted rate. At Sky Handling Partner, we recognise that our people are our greatest asset. That's why we invest in creating a supportive, inclusive, and rewarding workplace where every employee can thrive.

title: Check-in Agent
description: **At Sky Handling Partner, our people are our business.** We are delighted to offer an exciting opportunity to join our team as a Check\-in Agent at Dublin Airport. In this customer\-facing role, you will be at the heart of the airport operation, ensuring passengers receive a seamless and positive experience throughout their journey. From check\-in to boarding, you will play a key role in delivering exceptional service while supporting our airline clients in a fast\-paced and dynamic environment. No two days are the same, making this the perfect opportunity for individuals who enjoy working with people and thrive in a busy operational setting. **The Role** As a Check\-in with Sky Handling Partner, you will work closely with colleagues across multiple departments, Dublin Airport personnel, and airline representatives to ensure the smooth and efficient delivery of passenger services. Strong teamwork and communication are essential, as you will help manage passenger flows, support operational requirements, and respond effectively to any challenges that arise. By working collaboratively and maintaining a customer\-focused approach, you will contribute to a safe, efficient, and enjoyable travel experience for passengers while delivering excellent service to our airline partners. **Key Responsibilities** * Process passengers and their baggage for the correct flight in accordance with established procedures * Verify passenger documentation in line with immigration, security, and airline requirements * Ensure all required passenger security questions are asked in accordance with IAA directives * Assist disrupted passengers in line with airline procedures and the instructions of Supervisors and Duty Managers * Provide assistance to passengers who require special assistance or additional support * Manage the boarding process accurately, ensuring passenger numbers are correctly recorded and all safety and security procedures are followed * Deliver exceptional customer service in a professional, positive, and friendly manner at all times * Meet arriving passengers and direct or escort them through the appropriate immigration channels * Maintain the highest standards of grooming and presentation in accordance with company policy * Report any system errors, procedural issues, or operational concerns to your Supervisor * Exercise particular care to prevent duplicate check\-in errors * Undertake additional duties as required, including providing support to other operational departments **Person Specification** * Previous customer service experience * Ability to work effectively under pressure in a fast\-paced environment * Strong organisational, communication, and interpersonal skills * Flexibility to work rostered shifts and adapt to operational disruptions when required * Professional attitude with the ability to follow instructions from Duty Managers and the Station Manager * Excellent attendance, punctuality, and timekeeping record **Requirements** * Minimum Age Requirement \- 18 Years: Applicants must be at least 18 years of age due to the nature of the role and operational requirements within the airport environment * Successful Completion of a 5\-Year Security Background Check and Garda Vetting: All successful candidates must pass a comprehensive 5\-year background check and Garda Vetting process in line with aviation security regulations * Right to Live and Work in the European Union: Candidates must have the legal right to live and work within the European Union * Excellent Written and Verbal English: Strong communication skills are essential, including the ability to understand instructions, communicate effectively with passengers and colleagues, and complete documentation accurately * Flexibility to Work Rostered Shifts: This role operates on a shift basis and includes early mornings, late evenings, weekends, and public holidays. Flexibility is essential to support operational requirements and flight disruptions. Please note that shifts will primarily commence from 03:00 * Ability to Work Under Pressure: The airport environment is fast\-paced and dynamic. Candidates must be capable of remaining calm, professional, and organised while working in time\-sensitive situations * Excellent Attendance and Timekeeping: Reliability and punctuality are critical to maintaining operational efficiency and delivering a high standard of service to our airline clients and passengers * Commitment to SHP Grooming and Uniform Standards: Candidates must maintain a professional appearance and adhere to all company grooming and uniform requirements * Desirable Criteria * Manual Handling Certificate * Previous experience working within an operational environment * Previous customer service experience * Contract and Hours * Join a dedicated and collaborative team at Sky Handling Partner, where your commitment to safety, teamwork, and customer service will be recognised and rewarded * This is a permanent position with a six\-month probationary period. The role offers a competitive hourly rate of €14\.79 \- €14\.94 (inclusive), with overtime paid at time and a half * The contract guarantees 20 hours per week; however, additional hours are regularly available based on operational requirements. Flexibility is essential, as aircraft delays, schedule changes, and passenger demand may require additional working hours * Shift lengths typically range from 4 to 10 hours and include early starts, late finishes, weekends, and public holidays, providing a varied and dynamic working environment **Benefits** At Sky Handling Partner, we are committed to supporting the wellbeing, development, and success of our employees. We offer a range of benefits designed to support both your professional growth and personal wellbeing, including: * Career Development Opportunities \- Ongoing training and opportunities for progression within the aviation industry * Pension Scheme \- Helping you plan and prepare for your future * Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) \- Access to 24/7 confidential support and wellbeing services * Sick Pay Scheme \- Providing additional support when you need it most * Bike to Work Scheme \- Encouraging healthier and more sustainable commuting options * Long Service Awards \- Recognising and rewarding commitment and dedication to the business * Discounted Insurance Policies \- Exclusive employee discounts through Marsh Insurance Brokers Ireland * Reduced\-Rate Employee Parking \- Convenient on\-site parking available at a discounted rate At Sky Handling Partner, we recognise that our people are our greatest asset. That's why we invest in creating a supportive, inclusive, and rewarding workplace where every employee can thrive.

title: Chemistry Expert (Masters/PhDs)
description: **Chemistry Expert (AI Training)** **About The Role** What if your years of chemistry expertise could directly shape how AI reasons through the most complex scientific problems in the world? We're looking for Chemistry Experts with advanced degrees to design, solve, and evaluate graduate\-level chemistry problems that help train cutting\-edge AI models to think like scientists. This is a fully remote, flexible contract role built for working researchers, postdocs, faculty, and industry scientists who want meaningful work on their own schedule. No prior AI experience needed — just deep chemistry knowledge and a commitment to scientific rigor. * Organization: Alignerr * Type: Hourly Contract * Location: Remote * Commitment: 10–40 hours/week **What You'll Do** * Design and solve advanced chemistry problems spanning organic, inorganic, physical, and computational chemistry * Review and evaluate AI\-generated scientific content for accuracy, depth, and reasoning quality * Develop complex, real\-world problem statements that challenge and improve AI model reasoning * Collaborate asynchronously with AI researchers and fellow domain experts * Ensure scientific rigor, clarity, and precision across all deliverables * Apply computational tools or coding skills where relevant to problem design and validation **Who You Are** * Hold a Master's or PhD in Chemistry or a closely related field * Deep expertise in at least one area: organic, inorganic, physical, or computational chemistry * Comfortable working with Python or computational chemistry software (e.g., Gaussian, VASP) * Exceptionally detail\-oriented with strong scientific communication skills * Self\-motivated and reliable when working independently and asynchronously * Fluent in English **Nice to Have** * Experience with data annotation, scientific evaluation, or quality assurance workflows * Background in AI, machine learning, or cheminformatics * Familiarity with molecular modeling, reaction mechanisms, or materials science * Experience writing for scientific publication or peer review **Why Join Us** * Work on cutting\-edge AI projects alongside leading research labs and AI teams * Fully remote and flexible — work when and where it suits you * Freelance autonomy with the structure of meaningful, task\-based scientific work * Apply your expertise to problems that directly influence how AI understands science * Potential for ongoing work and contract extension as new projects launch

title: Chemistry Specialist (Masters/PhDs)
description: **Chemistry Specialist (Masters/PhDs) \- AI Training** **About The Role** What if your years of chemistry expertise could directly shape how AI reasons through the most complex scientific problems in the world? We're looking for Chemistry Specialists with advanced degrees to design, solve, and evaluate graduate\-level chemistry problems that train cutting\-edge AI models to think like expert scientists. This is a fully remote, flexible contract role built for working chemists and researchers who want to apply their deep domain knowledge in a high\-impact, forward\-looking way. No AI background required — just serious chemistry expertise and a commitment to scientific rigor. * Organization: Alignerr * Type: Hourly Contract * Location: Remote * Commitment: 10–40 hours/week **What You'll Do** * Design and solve complex, real\-world chemistry problems spanning organic, inorganic, physical, or computational chemistry * Review and evaluate AI\-generated chemistry content for scientific accuracy, depth, and clarity * Craft challenging problem statements that push AI reasoning to graduate\-level standards * Collaborate asynchronously with AI researchers and fellow domain experts to improve model performance * Ensure all deliverables meet high standards of scientific rigor and precision * Work independently on task\-based assignments — fully on your own schedule **Who You Are** * Master's or PhD in Chemistry or a closely related field * Deep expertise in at least one of: organic, inorganic, physical, or computational chemistry * Comfortable working with technical concepts at a graduate or research level * Naturally detail\-oriented with strong written communication skills in English * Self\-motivated and reliable when working independently * Experience coding in Python or using computational chemistry tools (e.g., Gaussian, VASP) is a strong plus **Nice to Have** * Prior experience with data annotation, content evaluation, or quality assurance workflows * Familiarity with AI tools or language model evaluation as an end user * Background in academic research, scientific publishing, or curriculum development * Experience designing problem sets, exams, or instructional materials at a graduate level **Why Join Us** * Work on cutting\-edge AI projects alongside the world's leading research labs * Fully remote and flexible — work when and where it suits you * Apply your advanced chemistry knowledge in a meaningful, intellectually stimulating way * Freelance autonomy with the structure of impactful, task\-based work * Contribute to AI systems that will shape how science is understood and communicated at scale * Potential for ongoing work and contract extension as new projects launch

title: Childcare Educator full time
description: We have a full time childcare educator position available to join our expanding team at juniors crèche, we are open 25 years in the Blanchardstown, littlepace area and expanding , minimum level 5 qualification, experience not necessary as full training will be given We are Looking for a fun loving enthusiastic practitioner to join the juniors team to educate and care for the children in our wobbler room. We have a great team at juniors ,which has been in the community since 2001, where we work and support each other. We also Ensure the safety, security and well being of all children in their care. We give full support but we will encourage you to plan and implement our appropriate stimulating program of activities underpinned by Aistear and Siolta with lots of support and help when you start and throughout from our team. You will be trained to do observations of your key children on a monthly basis too All training will be given in this room when you start and you will have continuous support after too Job Type: Full\-time Pay: Up to €17\.00 per hour Benefits: * Childcare * Company events * Employee discount * On\-site parking * Sick pay Ability to commute/relocate: * DUBLIN 15, County Dublin: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Education: * Junior Certificate (preferred) Experience: * Child care: 1 year (required) Language: * English (required) Licence/Certification: * Level 5 in childcare (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Civil & Structural Engineer
description: Arcadis is the world's leading company delivering sustainable design, engineering, and consultancy solutions for natural and built assets. We are more than 34,000 people, in over 30 countries, dedicated to improving quality of life. Everyone has an important role to play. With the power of many curious minds, together we can solve the world’s most complex challenges and deliver more impact together. We are looking for highly motivated Civil \& Structural Engineer to join our growing **Mobility** **Rail Civils Team** , based in our Dublin head office. This role offers the opportunity to work on a diverse range of multidisciplinary rail and infrastructure projects, supporting the continued growth of our rail capability across Ireland and the wider region. You will be part of a collaborative and supportive working environment, with opportunities to broaden your technical experience, expand your project portfolio and progress towards professional accreditation (IEng / CEng). Flexible and hybrid working arrangements are available. As part of our continued success in work winning and project delivery, we are expanding our capacity and capability to further strengthen our industry‑leading reputation in civil and structural engineering for rail and transport infrastructure. **Job Purpose** Working under the guidance of experienced engineers and project managers, you will contribute to the delivery of civil and structural engineering design tasks across multidisciplinary rail projects. You will support the production and coordination of design outputs in line with Arcadis Business Management Systems, helping to achieve project objectives for time, quality and budget while delivering high‑quality solutions for our clients. **Key Accountabilities** * Contribute to the production of civil and structural design deliverables of varying complexity, appropriate to your experience level. * Support project delivery teams across multiple stages of the design lifecycle, including concept, design development, detailed design and construction support. * Assist with the preparation, checking and coordination of drawings, calculations, reports and other design outputs in accordance with project and quality requirements. * Work collaboratively within multidisciplinary teams to coordinate design tasks, interfaces and workflows. * Support the application of innovative design approaches, digital engineering tools and the adoption of BIM‑enabled delivery. * Contribute to health \& safety and safe‑by‑design principles, particularly within rail and safety‑critical environments. * Engage in continuous professional development and technical learning under the supervision of senior team members. **Qualifications \& Experience** Applicants should demonstrate understanding and experience in **some or all** of the following areas: * Degree in Civil Engineering or Civil \& Structural Engineering. * Awareness or experience of rail and/or transport infrastructure projects, including exposure to safety‑critical environments. * Knowledge of relevant UK and Irish railway standards, Eurocodes and associated technical guidance. * Experience or familiarity with industry‑standard structural analysis and design software. * Basic knowledge of BIM processes and experience with CAD / BIM tools, supporting BIM Level 2 delivery. * Experience supporting design activities across a range of project stages (concept, design development, detailed design and construction support). * Some experience of coordinating with other disciplines to gather information and incorporate interfaces into design outputs. * Progression through, or commitment to, a recognised professional training scheme (Engineers Ireland, ICE, IStructE or similar), with a clear ambition to achieve IEng or CEng status. **Skills \& Attributes** * Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills. * Ability to work effectively within multidisciplinary teams. * Clear written and verbal communication skills. * Proactive approach to learning, development and quality. * Commitment to Arcadis values, health \& safety, sustainability and ethical engineering practice. **Why Arcadis?** We can only achieve our goals when everyone is empowered to be their best. We believe everyone's contribution matters. It’s why we are pioneering a skills\-based approach, where you can harness your unique experience and expertise to carve your career path and maximize the impact we can make together. You’ll do meaningful work, and no matter what role, you’ll be helping to deliver sustainable solutions for a more prosperous planet. Make your mark, on your career, your colleagues, your clients, your life and the world around you. Together, we can create a lasting legacy. **Join Arcadis. Create a Legacy.** **Our Commitment to Equality, Diversity, Inclusion \& Belonging** We want you to be able to bring your best self to work every day which is why we take equality and inclusion seriously and hold ourselves to account for our actions. Our ambition is to be an employer of choice and provide a great place to work for all our people. We believe that by working together diverse people with different experiences develop the most innovative ideas. Equality, diversity and inclusion is at the heart of how we improve quality of life and we work closely with our people across six ED\&I Workstreams: Age, Disability, Faith, Gender, LGBT\+ and Race. A diverse and skilled workforce is essential to our success.

title: Civil Design Engineer
description: Civil Design Engineer **Build the Future of Power with Us! Your skills will contribute to impactful projects that shape the future.** **If you can turn complex ideas into practical designs, we want you on our team. At Powercomm, your work wont just sit in a file; it will shape the infrastructure of tomorrow.** **Job Summary:** We are seeking a talented Civil Design Engineer to join our growing team and play a key role in delivering integrated Civil and Electrical design services to clients across the Electrical and Renewable Engineering sector. This excellent opportunity offers the chance to contribute to diverse projects including substations, solar parks, wind farms, and battery storage facilities. As a Civil Design Engineer, you will support the development and expansion of our small civil design team while delivering quality design solutions. This role combines technical design expertise with team development responsibilities, providing an ideal platform for a motivated engineer to advance their career while helping to build capability within a growing design practice. **Job Responsibilities:** * Prepare and review Civil/Structural drawings in AutoCAD. Someone with 3D Modelling(Revit) experience is preferred. * Carry out and check Design calculations of civil works, including drainage design and site development works. * Produce technical report, specifications, and material schedules. * Coordinate with MEP engineers, contractors, clients, and other project stakeholders. * Support construction with technical queries, site visits, as\-built drawings, and handover documentation. * Train and mentor Graduate Design Engineers, overseeing their work, and checking their calculations. **Job Requirements:** * BSc/MSc in Civil/Structural Engineering or equivalent with post\-graduate design experience with an engineering consultancy. * Building structure design experience using all the main materials on a variety of structural projects but particular emphasis on Electrical Infrastructure, Transformer bunds, Foundations for Electrical Switchgear, new and existing Substation Buildings, Drainage, Roadways and Cable Basements. * Knowledge of Eurocodes and Irish/UK standards. * Experience with AutoCAD software and Revit. Experience with drainage software is often advantageous. * Knowledge of finite element and frame analysis software for steel and RC structure design work experience with Robot Structural Analysis software is preferred but not mandatory. * Level 8 degree in relevant field (Level 9 in advantageous). * Chartered status preferred. * 3\+Years of relevant experience

title: Cleaner
description: **About The Company:** OCS UK \& Ireland is a leading facilities management company with 50,000\+ colleagues and a turnover in excess of £2bn. We deliver innovative, award\-winning services within facilities management, hard services, cleaning, security and catering. Our mission is to make people and places the best they can be for our colleagues, customers and the communities we serve. Our commitment to doing business the right way is rooted in our TRUE values \- **Trust, Respect, Unity, and Empowerment.** **Why Work for OCS?** **✅** **Award\-Winning Employer** : Ranked 36th on Glassdoor’s Best Companies to Work For 2025 — we value and motivate our people. **🚀** **Digital Learning** : The OCS Academy offers digital courses and resources to help you build skills and grow your career. 🙂 **Retail Perks** With our Hapi app, you can gain access to exclusive discounts, rewards and wellbeing resources. **📝** **Professional Growth** : 600\+ live learners across UK\&I — Empowering colleagues with further development and qualifications! **💰** **Flexible Pay** : Access a portion of earned wages before payday with our Wagestream App! (Contract Specific) **About The Role:** **Days of Working** : Monday, Tuesday, Thursday, Friday \- 9am to 10:45am As a Cleaner at OCS, you will play a crucial role in providing safe, clean environments in which our customers can thrive in their workspaces. Benefits for working with us: * Free Online Doctor Consultations via Web\-doctor * Competitive Salary * Contributory Pension after qualifying period * 21 Days Annual Leave increasing with service to 26 Days * Additional Leave Day on your Birthday after 1 year service * Staff Benefits/Discount Programme * Employee Assistance Programme * Training and Development Opportunities * Free onsite Parking **Your key responsibilities will include, but are not limited to:** * Tidying up work areas, cleaning floors and communal areas. * Vacuuming, sweeping, mopping, emptying bins and other tasks as required. * You may come into regular contact with customers so you must be able to assist them if needed. * You must complete all relevant Health \& Safety records and attend training courses when needed. **The ideal candidate should meet the following criteria:** * You must have Right to Work in the EU * A background in cleaning would be advantageous. * Attention to detail and thoroughness in completing tasks. **How to Apply:** If you are interested in joining a business that encourages professional development, career progression and the chance to work with inspirational people then please click apply and register your interest! You can also sign up to our job alerts when registering which will notify you when other suitable vacancies are available. We are an equal opportunities employer and rely on a diverse workforce with a broad range of knowledge, skills, and backgrounds to deliver our goals. We offer an inclusive and welcoming environment and actively encourage applications from all individuals regardless of race, gender, nationality, religion, sexual orientation, disability, or age.

title: Client Account Administrator
description: **Line of Service** Internal Firm Services **Industry/Sector** Not Applicable **Specialism** IFS \- Internal Firm Services \- Other **Management Level** Associate **Job Description \& Summary** At PwC, our people in business services and support focus on providing efficient and effective administrative support to enable smooth operations within the organisation. This includes managing schedules, coordinating meetings, and handling confidential information. Those in client administration at PwC will focus on managing and coordinating client relationships, prioritising smooth communication and efficient service delivery. You will utilise strong organisational skills and attention to detail to support the overall client experience. Driven by curiosity, you are a reliable, contributing member of a team. In our fast\-paced environment, you are expected to adapt to working with a variety of clients and team members, each presenting varying challenges and scope. Every experience is an opportunity to learn and grow. You are expected to take ownership and consistently deliver quality work that drives value for our clients and success as a team. As you navigate through the Firm, you build a brand for yourself, opening doors to more opportunities. Examples of the skills, knowledge, and experiences you need to lead and deliver value at this level include but are not limited to: * Apply a learning mindset and take ownership for your own development. * Appreciate diverse perspectives, needs, and feelings of others. * Adopt habits to sustain high performance and develop your potential. * Actively listen, ask questions to check understanding, and clearly express ideas. * Seek, reflect, act on, and give feedback. * Gather information from a range of sources to analyse facts and discern patterns. * Commit to understanding how the business works and building commercial awareness. * Learn and apply professional and technical standards (e.g. refer to specific PwC tax and audit guidance), uphold the Firm's code of conduct and independence requirements. Our commitment to you We’re committed to building an inclusive culture that empowers our people to thrive and feel a sense of belonging at PwC. We seek to attract talented people from different backgrounds with diverse perspectives who can bring innovation and creativity. At PwC, your hard work doesn't go unnoticed. We celebrate your achievements not just within the firm, but among your peers as well. Our rewards are designed to fit your unique lifestyle and needs . And because being part of PwC should be rewarding every day, you'll have access to PwC perks a handpicked selection of exclusive discounts, experiences, and extras just for being part of our team. Application support We are committed to an inclusive recruitment experience. If you require any adjustments during the application or interview process, please let us know ( ie\_experiencedhireteam@pwc.com ). All requests will be treated confidentially and supported wherever possible to help you showcase your strengths. **Education** *(if blank, degree and/or field of study not specified)* Degrees/Field of Study required: Degrees/Field of Study preferred: **Certifications** *(if blank, certifications not specified)* **Required Skills** **Optional Skills** Accepting Feedback, Accepting Feedback, Active Listening, Administrative Support, Business Administration, Business Analysis, Business Information System, Business Process Improvement, Business Process Management (BPM), Clerical Support, Client Interaction, Client Management, Client Onboarding, Communication, Communications Management, Confidential Information Handling, CRM Software, Customer Relationship Management, Data Analysis and Interpretation, Data Entry, Data Interpretation, Data Processing, Document Management, Emotional Regulation, Empathy {\+ 21 more} **Desired Languages** *(If blank, desired languages not specified)* **Travel Requirements** **Available for Work Visa Sponsorship?** **Government Clearance Required?** **Job Posting End Date**

title: Commercial Graduate Rotation Program - Benelux Market
description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* **About Futureforce University Recruiting** Our Futureforce University Recruiting program is dedicated to attracting, retaining and cultivating talent. Our interns and new graduates work on real projects that affect how our business runs, giving them the opportunity to make a tangible impact on the future of our company. With offices all over the world, our recruits have the chance to collaborate and connect with fellow employees on a global scale. We offer job shadowing, mentorship programs, talent development courses, and much more. **Job Category** Sales **Job Details** **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. **Fixed\-Term Contract** **Duration:** 12\-month **Start Date: Aug 2026** The EMEA Graduate Rotation Program is a new and exciting 12 month program which welcomes ambitious graduates who would like to begin their career journey within sales and solution engineering. It is a rotational program designed to give our graduates holistic learning and practical experience across both sales development and solution engineering. Having built the foundational skills and passed all required eligibility and readiness criteria, our graduates can then go on to pursue one of these two clear career paths. Our Top Graduates can potentially promote to the career path of their choice, (depending on business demand) by month 7 of the program. Our graduate program is a fully immersive program combining comprehensive learning paths, in\-role experience, customer interaction and mentorship from senior sales and solution engineering professionals. At Salesforce, you will have the opportunity to lay the foundations for a successful career in sales \& solution engineering and accelerate your career at a fast pace. We are a proud member of *Fortune 100* *Best Places to Work* list for the past 12 years and a consistent winner of *Great Places to Work.* ****Who are we looking for?**** * You are fluent in English \& Dutch * You are a 2024 or 2025 graduate (minimum bachelor’s degree). We consider applicants from All Academic Fields * You are interested in the impact technology can have on the world and business * You are a creative problem\-solver * You are a relationship builder and possess a growth mindset * Are curious, self\-motivated and focused on continuous learning * You thrive working in a team environment * You have excellent interpersonal skills * You are goal\-oriented and comfortable with a fast\-paced environment **What Does Our New EMEA Graduate Program Look Like** The Program is structured as follows: **Introduction Month** During your first month you will have extensive onboarding enablement in a Salesforce\-like academy, where you will: * Get immersed in the culture of salesforce and truly understand our values * Develop an understanding of our solutions, technologies and tools. * Learn critical professional skills for your career in salesforce * Structured learning calendar with sessions \& hands\-on workshops led by experts and program alumni **Rotations** During this Graduate Program you will have the opportunity to experience **the Sales Graduate Path** and **the Solution Engineering Graduate Path.** The structure for both are similar, for both you will spend 60% of your time immersed in the role, you will have clear tasks, clear targets and you will align with key stakeholders and the other 40% of your time will be spent on role specific learning. You will be paired with mentors/buddies with experience in the path. If during your first rotation (months 2 throughout to month 4\) are on the Solution Engineering Graduate Rotation, then you will join the Sales Graduate Rotation (from month 5 through to 7\) and vice versa. So in the these 6 months you will have exposure to both roles. Your time spent mastering the fundamentals of both these roles during these rotations will empower you to be even more productive and impactful when you eventually promote. Throughout the rotations there will be self paced certification and skills assessment. You will be required to meet all readiness eligibility criteria before you can Panel for Promotion. **An Example of what you can expect during the specific rotations** **The Sales Graduate Rotation** * You will be taught salesforce’s best in class prospecting and qualification methodologies * You will learn how to use social, digital, video, chat and phone platforms to effectively engage with Salesforce prospects * You will collaborative with Sales Development Specialists to run campaigns and help build sales pipeline for the EMEA Sales organisation **The Solution Engineer Graduate Rotation** * You will have the opportunity to learn our solutions at a deeper more technical level * You work closely with the solution engineering organisation supporting them in building cutting edge cutomer demonstrations * You will learn the art of customer discovery \& how to create a customer point of view ****What’s in it for you?**** * A clear and structured career path within our Sales and Solution Engineering organisation * Opportunity to work in an international team, within a nurturing environment * Opportunity to join our diverse internal equality groups * Comprehensive benefits package * Relocation budget and guidance in your relocation\-process * Additional seven days paid time off for charitable causes \- check out our 1/1/1 model ****Map Your Future**** With our Graduate Program there are two optional career paths * The Sales Path where after your Grad Program Rotations you can progress to Sales Development Representative, Business Development Representative and then into our Sales organisation as Account Executive, where you will position our solutions to potential customers. In some instances, this path may vary or be subject to change. * The Solution Engineering path where after your Grad Program Rotations you can progress to Associate Solution Engineer and rise to work at every customer level ESB, SMB, GRB. In some instances, this path may vary or be subject to change. * With our strong culture of mentoring we strive to develop industry\-leading sales and solution engineering leaders. This is a phenomenal opportunity to develop your international experience in an organisation that has transformed the Enterprise Software Market with Cloud Computing ****Salesforce in Dublin**** When we opened our first office in Ireland in 2000 we were one of the first digital companies to locate here. Today, our Dublin office has grown into a leading technology hub, and Salesforce is the global leader in customer relationship management (CRM), bringing companies of every size and industry together with their customers. Our new Salesforce Tower Dublin will bring our dynamic, multinational team together in the vibrant Silicon Docks area. Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. Accommodations If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form. Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options. ********Posting Statement******** Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non\-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. title: Commissioning Engineer
description: **Commissioning Engineer – Dublin** (Nationwide Travel Required) Design Build Search is currently seeking a **Commissioning Engineer** based in the Dublin/Meath/Kildare area to join our clients growing team. The company delivers projects nationwide, and the successful candidate will be comfortable with regular travel across Ireland as required. This is a fantastic opportunity for a recent electrical engineer graduate looking to become part of an established, close\-knit team with a strong reputation for delivering high\-quality projects. **Responsibilities** * Commission smoke and natural ventilation systems in accordance with project specifications and EN12101 standards. * Interface with BMS systems via Modbus/BACnet gateways. * Configure and test D\+H control panels (CPS\-M), actuators (e.g., CDC series), and accessories. * Perform site acceptance tests and final handover inspections with clients and consultants. * Produce commissioning reports, operation manuals, and compliance documentation. * Collaborate with project managers and installation teams to ensure timely delivery. * Assist in fault finding and on\-site technical support as required. **Requirements** * Electrical Engineering qualification (Level 6\+ or equivalent). * 1 year of experience commissioning ventilation or building automation systems is desired * Strong understanding of electrical schematics and low\-voltage systems (24V DC). * Experience with control panels, smoke ventilation, or natural ventilation systems preferred. * Familiarity with EN12101\-2 / EN12101\-10 compliance is an advantage. * Valid Safe Pass and Manual Handling * Full clean driving licence if you’re interested in learning more, apply with your CV today for a confidential discussion.

title: Compliance Analyst
description: **Title** : Compliance Analyst **Location** : Dublin, Ireland **Reporting to:** Senior Compliance Manager **Contract Type** : Full time – Permanent **Role Summary** The Compliance Analyst will report to a Senior Compliance Manager on the Compliance team. They will be responsible for carrying out periodic tests in line with the Compliance monitoring plan. Testing will include key investor protection areas under MIFID II such as Suitability, Appropriateness, Product Governance and Client Categorisation among other areas. The Analyst will have the opportunity to interact with a wide range of business stakeholders in the role and provide compliance feedback and recommendations. The Analyst will present their work to the team at regular compliance monitoring meetings and contribute to enhancing the Cantor Fitzgerald investor protection framework on an ongoing basis. The Analyst will be responsible for reviewing marketing materials and will assist with other Compliance related tasks as required. Under The Central Bank of Ireland’s (CBI) Individual Accountability Framework (IAF) established under The Central Bank (Individual Accountability Framework) Act 2023 (IAF Act), as an expected Control Function holder, you have a Duty of Responsibility under the CBI Common Conduct Standards. These standards include: * acting with honesty and integrity, * acting with due skill, care and diligence, * co\-operating in good faith and without delay with the Central Bank of Ireland * acting in the best interest of customers and treating them fairly and professionally * operating in compliance with standards of market conduct and trading venue rules **Key Responsibilities** * Execute monitoring tasks in line with the Compliance Monitoring Plan * Prepare output reports detailing observations, findings and recommendations for review by Senior Manager * Issuance of final reports to relevant first line functions and ensure findings are remediated where needed and brought to resolution * Present monitoring findings and recommendations as required * Prepare relevant management information for periodic Committee and Board reporting * Assist the Senior Manager in review and updating of compliance policies and procedures * Assist in the delivery of compliance training programme for the first line of defence * Asist in preparing regulatory responses to the Central Bank of Ireland * Review marketing materials prepared by the business * Assist with other Compliance tasks as required. **Skills \& Competencies** * Has a “can do” attitude and takes ownership of an issue from start to finish. * Offers practical advice to the business. * Uses experience, knowledge, and judgement to influence and deliver upon objectives. * Builds a sense of teamwork on his or her team and within/between groups. * Strives to improve him/herself and the Firm and actively seeks feedback from peers and management. * Has a willingness to grow within the Firm and to take on new responsibilities as required. **Requirements** * Third Level Qualification preferable. * Microsoft Office skills essential with strong Microsoft Excel skills a requirement. * Financial Services experience * Experience of Compliance Monitoring / Assurance Testing preferred * LCI qualification (or agreement to work towards same). **Please note that under the Central Bank of Ireland’s Fitness and Probity Standards, this role is a Control Function 2 (CF2\) role \- Ensuring, controlling, or monitoring *compliance* by a regulated financial service provider with its relevant obligations**

title: Compliance, Asset Management Compliance, Associate, Dublin
description: **Job Description** **OUR IMPACT** Our division prevents, detects and mitigates compliance, regulatory and reputational risk across the firm and helps to strengthen the firm’s culture of compliance. Compliance accomplishes these through the firm’s enterprise\-wide compliance risk management program. As an independent control function and part of the firm’s second line of defense, Compliance assesses the firm’s compliance, regulatory and reputational risk; monitors for compliance with new or amended laws, rules and regulations; designs and implements controls, policies, procedures and training; conducts independent testing; investigates, surveils and monitors for compliance risks and breaches; and leads the firm’s responses to regulatory examinations, audits and inquiries. You'll be part of a team with members from a wide range of academic and professional backgrounds, such as law, accounting, sales, and trading. We look for those who possess sound judgment, curiosity, and are able to adapt to a changing regulatory landscape. **YOUR IMPACT** This is a role on the Asset Wealth Management(“AWM”) Compliance Advisory team helping to provide compliance advisory services covering portfolio management, trading, sales and marketing activity within Goldman Sachs Asset Management (“GSAM”) and based in Dublin. The successful candidate will work with business partners, Legal and Compliance colleagues in connection with helping to respond to regulatory requests, respond to compliance queries from the business, uplift compliance program in response to regulatory reform, perform surveillance and forensic reviews, train the business in compliance matters, participate in policy updates and drafting, conduct marketing material sign off, and advise on sales activity. **Experience** * Ability to work as part of a global team as well as act as project lead on various compliance matters * Familiar with the risk and compliance function of asset management * Understanding the development and implementation of compliance programs * Ability to deal with business personnel and deliver good compliance outcomes in time constrained situations **Responsibilities** * Provide Compliance support and representation on AWM strategic initiatives * Review and approval of financial promotion materials * Provide daily compliance surveillance of GSAM portfolios for regulatory requirements and applicable policies and procedures * Assistance in the production of board reports * Participate in the review and evaluation of new instruments and vehicles to identify compliance issues and surveillance needs * Develop and draft compliance policies and procedures, outlining firm policies, regulations and best practices * Ensure breaches and complaints are logged and cover the compliance aspect of the sign off * Create compliance training programs * Compliance advisory regarding global asset management marketing and distribution questions: UCITS/Non\-UCITS/cross border distribution * Review and approval of due diligence on third party distributors * Compliance advisory on new GSAM fund launch projects * Preparation, organisation and delivery of compliance trainings * Miscellaneous projects as required **Qualifications** * 3 years regulatory risk or compliance experience (asset management experience preferred) * Highly motivated risk and control focused individual with excellent judgment * Knowledge of investment instruments including derivatives * General compliance knowledge and familiarity with CBI and FCA regulations, MiFID, UCITS, AIFMD and SFDR * Effective analytical and organizational skills * Strong verbal and written communication skills * Ability to handle multiple tasks with attention to detail and work under pressure * Ability to work both independently and as part of a team **About Goldman Sachs** At Goldman Sachs, we commit our people, capital and ideas to help our clients, shareholders and the communities we serve to grow. Founded in 1869, we are a leading global investment banking, securities and investment management firm. Headquartered in New York, we maintain offices around the world. We believe who you are makes you better at what you do. We're committed to fostering and advancing diversity and inclusion in our own workplace and beyond by ensuring every individual within our firm has a number of opportunities to grow professionally and personally, from our training and development opportunities and firmwide networks to benefits, wellness and personal finance offerings and mindfulness programs. Learn more about our culture, benefits, and people at GS.com/careers. We’re committed to finding reasonable accommodations for candidates with special needs or disabilities during our recruiting process. title: Conservation Architect
description: **Architects (x2\) – Conservation Projects \| Dublin URGENT** **Shape the Future of Ireland’s Past** IT Staff is excited to be recruiting two **Architects** to join a major Irish government agency responsible for some of the nation’s most iconic buildings. From **Dublin Castle** to the **Custom House** , the **Four Courts** , the **National Museum of Ireland** and more, this is a rare chance to make a lasting impact on Ireland’s architectural heritage. **What You’ll Be Doing** No two days will be the same. You’ll be: * Leading **conservation, refurbishment, fit\-out, and maintenance projects** on landmark State\-owned buildings. * Collaborating with **specialist craftspeople** on historic joinery, plasterwork, and metalwork. * Producing **high\-quality AutoCAD drawings** and contract documentation. * Driving projects from concept through to completion, both in the office and **on\-site management** . * Working alongside a skilled design team to safeguard and reimagine some of Ireland’s most treasured structures. **What We’re Looking For** * At least **5 years’ post\-graduation experience** , including project/site management. * Strong design and drawing skills, with **proficiency in AutoCAD** . * A genuine **passion for historic buildings** and an eagerness to contribute to their conservation. * Conservation experience is a real advantage. **Education \& Experience** Ideally, you’ll bring: * A postgraduate qualification in building conservation **and/or** at least 2 years’ experience working with historic buildings, monuments, or protected structures. **Extra Points If You Have:** * Experience on **public or government buildings** . * Hands\-on involvement in building conservation projects. * A proven ability to work in multi\-disciplinary teams and deliver results. **Why Apply** * **Contract length:** Initial 1\-year contract with extensions up to 3–4 years. * **Flexibility:** Hybrid working options available (Dublin or Trim base). * **Balance:** 37\.4 \-hour work week, Monday to Friday. * **Impact:** Your work will directly shape the conservation of some of Ireland’s most important cultural and historic landmarks. **Interested** This is your chance to combine your **architectural expertise** with a **deep cultural mission** . If this sounds like the next step in your career, we’d love to hear from you. Please forward your most up\-to\-date CV for consideration.

description: **Company Description** Wix makes it possible for anyone to succeed online. Since 2006, we’ve grown to around 5,500 employees in 22 countries, launched over 40 products, and serve over 278 million users and their visitors worldwide. At Wix, we push you to innovate, evolve in non\-traditional ways, and collaborate. We operate in small teams that work closely together at a high level. This same vision defines us as an employer: creating a work environment where everyone is welcome, and anyone has the right to succeed.tunity employer. Wix was built around the idea that everyone has the right to be successful, online. This same vision defines us as an employer: creating a work environment where everyone is welcome, and anyone has the right to succeed. **Job Description** As a Customer Care Expert at Wix, you’ll be responsible for helping our over 278 million users worldwide make their online dreams a reality. As part of our global organization of Customer Care, we’re responsible for building solid relationships with Wix users, uncovering their business needs, and guiding them to success. Working with us, you’ll find a friendly atmosphere, fantastic coworkers, and diverse growth opportunities in a dynamic environment. We’re spread across the globe and offer an office, remote, or hybrid work model depending on the site. We currently have this shifts available for the role: Monday \- Friday 11am\-8pm This role is available under two flexible options: * **Hybrid:** Working from our Dublin office a minimum of 2 days per week, with the flexibility to work from home the remaining days. * **Remote:** 100% remote, available to candidates permanently residing anywhere within the Republic of Ireland. We currently only accept applicants who are located in Ireland and eligible to work full\-time in the Republic Of Ireland

We move fast, stay close to our users, and focus on the work that drives the most impact. We're one of the fastest growing marketplaces and were recently named the \#1 Best Startup Employer in America by Forbes. Check out the latest Whatnot updates on our news and engineering blogs and join us as we enable anyone to turn their passion into a business and bring people together through commerce. **💻 Role** The Customer Experience team is responsible for driving the best possible user experience through process improvement, order management, and problem solving. As part of this team you will be tasked with doing anything possible to make sure our customers get the product they ordered as fast as possible. And if something went wrong, you are ensuring that we are doing everything possible to make it right. * Interact with buyers and sellers with a customer first attitude ensuring a positive experience * Resolve all customer issues regarding payments, orders, shipments and general questions with a high quality, and in a timely manner * Become an expert in the Whatnot product, processes and systems to drive positive outcomes for our users * Work with other departments to troubleshoot, research and resolve open questions * Seek out opportunities to eliminate repeat contacts and improve the overall customer experience **We offer flexibility to work from home or from one of our global office hubs, and we value in\-person time for planning, problem\-solving, and connection. Team members in this role must live within commuting distance of our Dublin hub.** **👋 You** People who do well at Whatnot tend to be comfortable figuring things out as they go, biased toward action, and genuinely curious about what they're building. They care more about outcomes than credit and stay close to the product and the people using it. **Skills** A hungry individual looking to jump start their career in a fast paced environment. Someone willing to take on any role or task, learn new skills and be a part of building a great company. A customer service mentality with passion to reach the best possible outcome. Here’s what we’re hoping to see in your background: * 2\+ years of support experience handling email and/or chat services. * Weekend availability required * Fluent in English * Secondary language preferred, in particular French, German, or Dutch * Understanding of Ecommerce and Marketplace operations * Experience with Customer facing systems like Zendesk, Kustomer, or Intercom. * Obsession with customers / being customer first / serving customers * Proactive problem\-solver and process\-improver * Knowledge of Collectibles is a plus * Bachelor's degree preferred * Previous start\-up experience is a plus ****🎁 Benefits**** * Generous Holiday and Time off Policy * Health Insurance options including Medical, Dental, Vision * Work From Home Support + Home office setup allowance + Monthly allowance for cell phone and internet * Care benefits + Monthly allowance for wellness + Annual allowance towards Childcare + Lifetime benefit for family planning, such as adoption or fertility expenses * Retirement; 401k offering for Traditional and Roth accounts in the US (employer match up to 4% of base salary) and Pension plans internationally * Monthly allowance to dogfood the app + All Whatnauts are expected to develop a deep understanding of our product. We're passionate about building the best user experience, and all employees are expected to use Whatnot as both a buyer and a seller as part of their job (our dogfooding budget makes this fun and easy!). * Parental Leave + 16 weeks of paid parental leave \+ one month gradual return to work \*company leave allowances run concurrently with country leave requirements which take precedence. Please find our Whatnot Candidate Privacy Notice here. ****💛 EOE**** Whatnot is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. title: Data Analyst, Healthcare Economic
description: Optum is a global organization that delivers care, aided by technology to help millions of people live healthier lives. The work you do with our team will directly improve health outcomes by connecting people with the care, pharmacy benefits, data and resources they need to feel their best. Here, you will find a culture guided by diversity and inclusion, talented peers, comprehensive benefits and career development opportunities. Come make an impact on the communities we serve as you help us advance health equity on a global scale. Join us to start **Caring. Connecting. Growing together.** As a **Data Analyst** at Optum Health Ireland, you'll play a key role in the development and implementation of reporting and analytic approaches / tools to support clinical teams. The successful candidate will collaborate cross\-functionally with various business and data analytics teams, have proven business analysis experience, and the ability to manage multiple competing business projects at one time. Careers with Optum offer flexible work arrangements and individuals who live and work in the Republic of Ireland will have the opportunity to split their monthly work hours between our Dublin office and telecommuting from a home\-based office in a hybrid work model. **What You Will Do** * Assist the team in architecting solutions; Collaborate with stakeholders and internal business partners to understand business requirements and translate them into analytical solutions that meet business needs * Analyze and investigate solutions \- Contribute to the design, coding, testing, documentation, and maintenance of high\-quality and scalable Big Data solutions, delivered on time * With direction from the team, develop and provide insights and guidance into actionable analytic findings through clear, succinct written and oral summaries, tables, charts, reports or presentations, that can be understood by a variety of audiences * Identify gaps, process improvement, and automation opportunities, and make recommendations to help drive such activities to execution * Proven ability to gain subject matter expertise through individual research and self\-guided analyses * Utilize a broad range of analytic approaches including predictive analytics, issue detection, trend analysis, and data visualization to best address the business priorities * Comply with and contribute to departmental standards related to data, data governance, project planning, validation, and documentation You will be rewarded and recognized for your performance in an environment that will challenge you and give you clear direction on what it takes to succeed in your role, as well as providing development for other roles you may be interested in. **What You Will Bring** *If you don't meet every single requirement, don't let that hold you back \- we're just as excited about potential as we are about qualifications and experience, and you could be exactly who we're looking for.* * Bachelor's Degree in relevant field (data science, analytics, mathematics, economics, business, statistics, engineering, or finance) or equivalent experience * Proven experience with statistical models, demonstrate understanding of different statistical methods and approaches * Proven clearly demonstrated expertise in problem solving, data management, study design, and study implementation * Proven exposure to Machine Learning or AI model development and deployment * Proven solid interpersonal skills and ability to interact collaboratively with internal customers across multiple departments and business segments * Demonstrable experience with SQL, and at least one scripting language such as SAS, R, or Python * Demonstrable experience with Data Visualization / Dashboard tools (e.g., Power BI) **Other Useful Skills And Experience Include** * Proven experience or knowledge of cloud\-based data analytics e.g. (AWS, Azure, Snowflake) * Demonstrable creative problem solving and critical thinking * Proven adaptability and flexibility to thrive in a dynamic business environment * Proven experience in prioritizing work on multiple complex assignments with ongoing deadlines **What We Offer** * Opportunities for professional development * Inclusive and supportive team culture * Key benefits: Private health insurance, wellness programs, matching pension contribution, lunch provided by the company, training opportunities, employee donations matching and others **Please note you must currently be eligible to work and remain indefinitely without any restrictions in the country to title: Data Quality Analyst
description: **About us:** Amach is an industry\-leading technology driven company with headquarters located in Dublin and remote teams in UK and Europe. Our blended teams of local and nearshore talent are optimised to deliver high quality and collaborative solutions. Founded in 2013, Amach was created to solve a specific problem in aviation: too much complexity, too little usable intelligence. We help airlines modernise their operating model using cloud, data and Al\-delivered by teams with deep aviation domain expertise. Our goal is to maximize airlines' operational efficiency by optimizing resource use, reduce costs and increase customer experience and satisfaction. We are seeking a **Data Quality Analyst** to be embedded with our customer, where you will drive improvements in data quality, trust, and accountability across their organisation. Working closely with the customer's teams, you will identify critical data issues, define and monitor quality rules, investigate root causes, and partner with data owners and stewards to deliver sustainable improvements. This is a hands\-on role that combines data quality management, governance, and stakeholder engagement to support the customer's strategic data objectives. ***Please note** : this is a fully remote role.* **Key responsibilities \& duties include:** * Gather and document business requirements for data quality monitoring and governance initiatives working with the customer's stakeholders * Support the triage, prioritisation, investigation, and remediation of data quality issues and incidents across the customer's data landscape * Assist with the maintenance, adoption, and continuous improvement of the customer's enterprise data catalogue * Build strong working relationships with the customer's business stakeholders, data owners, and data stewards to support data quality improvement initiatives * Manage stakeholder communications throughout the lifecycle of data quality projects and initiatives with the customer's teams * Analyse the customer's existing databases, systems, and data sources to identify opportunities for improving data quality * Produce clear and comprehensive documentation, including business requirements, data quality rules, metrics, and reports * Collaborate closely with the customer's Agile delivery teams, including Product Owners, Data Engineers, and Product Managers * Contribute to the continuous improvement of the customer's data governance processes, standards, and operating procedures * Support governance activities relating to audit, risk, compliance, and regulatory requirements * Measure and report on data quality KPIs and business outcomes for the customer's leadership * Support the implementation and adoption of data governance policies and standards across the customer's organisation **Required Experience:** * Minimum 3\+ years' experience in a Data Analyst, Data Quality Analyst, Data Governance, or related role * Strong SQL and data analysis capabilities * Experience working with data quality management and data governance programmes * Experience identifying, investigating, and resolving data quality issues * Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills with the ability to influence and collaborate across multiple groups * Experience creating business and technical documentation * Ability to facilitate workshops and work effectively with both business and technical teams * Strong
