title: Incident Response Analyst 1
description: **Job Overview** The Incident Response Analyst is a key member of the Incident Response Center (IRC), responsible for 24/7 monitoring, first\-level analysis, triage, and escalation of alerts related to data center infrastructure and services. This role ensures continuous health, availability, and security of data center operations by monitoring critical systems, detecting events, assessing impact, and coordinating with cross\-functional teams for timely resolution. The Analyst provides real\-time situational awareness across data center facilities, network and server systems, environmental controls, cloud infrastructure, and external risk factors. **Key Responsibilities** * Monitor alarms and alerts across data center infrastructure, including network and server devices, hardware health indicators (disk usage, temperature, etc.), WAN circuits, local connectivity, and facility environmental systems such as temperature, humidity, power, racks, and PDUs. * Monitor Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI), cloud dashboards, and relevant monitoring tools for system health and service availability. * Assess external risks such as natural events (floods, earthquakes, fires, severe weather) that may impact data center operations. * Monitor and review CCTV activity for security events within data center facilities. * Validate, investigate, and analyze alerts to determine impact and urgency. * Create, update, and manage incident tickets using Jira or similar ITSM platforms. * Escalate incidents promptly to cross\-functional teams including Network, Compute, Storage, Facilities, Security, and Cloud teams. * Communicate incident updates and notifications using tools such as Everbridge and email. * Prepare Post\-Incident Reviews (PIRs), document timelines, and maintain accurate incident records and handover notes. * Review and track vendor emails regarding planned maintenance, emergency changes, and service advisories. * Maintain documentation including SOPs, process updates, and knowledge base articles. **Basic Qualifications** * Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, or related field, or equivalent practical experience. * 2–5 years of experience in a data center operations center, NOC/SOC, monitoring environment, or incident management role. * Basic understanding of network fundamentals (TCP/IP, routing, WAN/LAN concepts) and server or hardware components (CPU, memory, storage). * Ability to interpret system alerts, analyze issues, and perform initial triage. * Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to work independently, meet goals, and maintain attention to detail. * Demonstrated ability to interact effectively at all levels within the organization, including with clients, while being a collaborative team player. * Ability to work in 24/7 rotating shifts as required. **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience in a Data Center, NOC, SOC, or similar operations command center environment. * Familiarity with tools such as Jira, Everbridge, DCIM systems, monitoring platforms (SolarWinds, Nagios, Grafana, etc.), and CCTV monitoring tools. * Experience writing incident reports or post\-incident reviews. * Exposure to cloud platforms such as Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, AWS, Azure, or GCP. * Knowledge of ITIL processes, especially Incident, Problem, and Change Management. * Strong situational awareness and ability to manage multiple priorities during critical events. * Effective collaboration skills when working with cross\-functional support teams.

title: Information Security Business Analyst
description: **The Role:** Our client is looking for a Technical Business Analyst with strong security domain experience to lead requirements definition for a Managed SOC procurement. The role will focus on building a comprehensive business and technical requirements catalogue and supporting vendor evaluation and RFP development. **Required:** * Strong experience as a Business Analyst in security or SOC related programmes * Proven experience leading requirements workshops and documentation * Experience with RFP processes, vendor evaluation, or procurement support * Ability to translate business needs into technical requirements * Strong documentation discipline and attention to detail **Responsibilities:** **Requirements Definition \-** * Lead detailed business and technical requirements capture for a Managed SOC * Produce structured documentation including: * Business requirements * Functional and non functional requirements * Service and operating model requirements * Ensure requirements are clear, traceable, and signed off by stakeholders **Workshops and Stakeholder Engagement \-** * Plan and facilitate workshops across Cyber Security, TechOps and other stakeholders * Drive consensus on requirements, standards, and priorities * Challenge and refine requirements to avoid ambiguity or scope creep **Vendor Evaluation Support \-** * Support development of evaluation framework and scoring models * Analyse vendor responses and map back to requirements * Identify gaps, risks, and trade offs **RFP Development \-** * Contribute to creation of the full RFP pack * Ensure requirements are clearly articulated for vendor response * Support clarification cycles during procurement **Documentation and Governance \-** * Maintain requirements traceability and documentation standards * Support decision logs, evaluation outputs, and audit evidence **Desirable:** * Experience with SIEM, MDR, SOC operations or threat monitoring * Familiarity with enterprise security tooling landscape * Experience working in large, complex organisations

title: Information Technology Support Analyst
description: Our Dublin based client are looking for an **Information Technology Support Analyst** to join their team on a permanent basis You will have the opportunity to become part of an established and strong team and work for a company that is a market leader in their field There is an on site presence required for this role and the office is Dublin city centre based **Responsibilities:** * Provide technical support to end\-users and assist in resolving various IT issues in an effective and timely manner. * Learn to identify and resolve IT problems and issues as they arise * Troubleshoot hardware and software issues on site and via cloud services * Assist in the setup, configuration, and maintenance of end\-user devices, laptops and phones * Maintain a highly organised inventory of IT assets and resources (ITAM). * Assist in monitoring and tracking issues to prevent problems occurring a second or third time. * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to ensure escalations and tickets are registered and dealt with effectively. * Contribute to IT projects, including software deployments and hardware upgrades. * Escalation of technical issues to other team members when necessary and required **Skills and Requirements:** * Minimum of 2\-3 years of experience in a technical support role. * Experience with Active Directory, Azure AD, and managing security groups. * Ability to handle access requests and managing permissions. * Proven capabilities in troubleshooting Office 365 issues and software installations. * Proactive problem\-solving skills. * Have strong verbal and written communication skills. * Demonstrated teamwork skills, attention to detail, and a strong work ethic. * Enthusiasm for technology and a desire to learn and grow in the field. **HOW TO APPLY:** To apply for this role, please submit your updated CV. If your skills and experience match job requirements, you will be contacted to discuss your application in detail. **SIMILAR ROLES OPEN:** As a specialized recruitment agency, we also have several other permanent and contract IT roles available. Visit our website for more information or explore additional opportunities

title: Information Technology Support Engineer
description: **On\-Site IT Support Engineer – Dublin 13** **Location:** Dublin 13 – 5 days on\-site **Salary:** €35,000–€50,000 DOE We’re looking for a **hands\-on IT Support Engineer** to join our growing team in **Dublin 13** . This is a **full\-time, 5 days per week on\-site role** , ideal for someone who thrives on supporting users directly and keeping IT operations running smoothly. **What You’ll Do:** * Provide **L1 \& L2 IT support** for staff across hardware, software, and application issues. * Manage **device deployment, upgrades, and migrations** for Windows, macOS, and mobile devices. * Use **ITSM tools (ServiceNow mandatory)** to log, track, and resolve incidents, ensuring SLA compliance. * Support **cloud services, endpoint management, and networking troubleshooting** . * Collaborate with colleagues and escalate issues when needed, contributing to knowledge bases and best practices. **What We’re Looking For:** * **3–5 years’ experience** in a hands\-on IT support role (desktop, server, and cloud environments). * Proven experience with **ITSM tools** (ServiceNow required). * Strong **troubleshooting skills** across Windows, macOS, Linux, and mobile devices. * Familiarity with **Microsoft 365, Azure, Active Directory, Intune, and cloud services** . * Excellent **communication skills** , both with users and internal teams. * Positive, proactive, and motivated to support a dynamic on\-site IT environment. **Why Join Us:** * Work in a **structured, on\-site environment** with clear career progression. * Get exposure to **modern IT infrastructure and cloud technologies** . * Collaborate with a **friendly, professional team** and make an immediate impact. 📩 Apply now or share with someone who’d be perfect for this role!

title: Infrastructure Engineer
description: Our client is seeking an experienced Infrastructure Engineer to join their IT Service Operations team. This hands\-on role is responsible for supporting and enhancing core infrastructure, Microsoft 365 services, network operations, and security platforms. The successful candidate will play a key role in delivering secure, reliable, and high\-performing IT services while contributing to infrastructure projects and continuous improvement initiatives. **Key Responsibilities** * Administer and support Microsoft 365, including Exchange Online, Entra ID, and Intune. * Manage Windows and Linux servers, virtualisation platforms, and cloud infrastructure. * Support networking and firewall technologies, including Cisco and Palo Alto solutions. * Troubleshoot complex infrastructure, network, and Microsoft 365 issues. * Deliver infrastructure projects from planning through implementation. * Coordinate with external service providers and support partners to ensure effective service delivery. * Maintain security controls through monitoring, vulnerability management, and patching. * Conduct Disaster Recovery (DR) testing and maintain technical documentation. * Contribute to ongoing service improvements and operational excellence. **Qualifications \& Experience** * Third\-level qualification in Information Technology or a related discipline. * Minimum 5 years' experience in infrastructure engineering, systems engineering, or IT operations. * Strong Microsoft 365 administration and systems engineering experience. * Good understanding of networking, firewall technologies, cloud platforms, and security best practices. * Experience working within a regulated environment would be advantageous. **Desirable Certifications** * Azure Administrator Associate (AZ\-104\) * Intune Administrator (MD\-102\) * Cisco CCNA * Palo Alto PCNSA * CompTIA Security\+ * ITIL 4 Foundation **Key Competencies** * Customer Relationship Management * Planning and Organisation * Problem Solving and Decision Making * Strong Communication and Interpersonal Skills * Collaboration and Teamwork * Drive for Results **How to Apply** To be considered for this opportunity, please submit your latest CV.

title: Installer
description: Due to continued growth, we are seeking skilled and motivated **Architectural Metalwork Installers** and **Pergola Installers** to join our expanding team. **Role Summary** The successful candidate will be responsible for the safe, accurate, and high\-quality installation across a variety of commercial and residential sites. The role requires strong technical ability, attention to detail, and the professionalism to work independently or as part of a site team while meeting agreed timelines and company standards. **Key Responsibilities** * Read, interpret, and work from technical drawings, plans, specifications, and job instructions. * Install balustrade systems (glass, aluminium, steel or composite) and external/internal cladding systems to a high standard. * Ensure all works are completed safely and in accordance with company procedures, industry standards, and Health \& Safety regulations. * Carry out site preparation, setting out, fixing, alignment, and finishing works with precision and attention to detail. * Communicate effectively with supervisors, colleagues, and clients, raising any site title: Junior Associate - Investment Servicing
description: Summary: The Junior Associate will assist the Middle Office team in the development of large\-scale projects for high profile platforms, from Management Companies to fund clients and work Subscriptions and Redemption activity, Unfilled Order and Pending Instructions, Failed Trades, New Order Instructions and Reconciliations. * Assist the Manager with the design and implementation of Actively participate in team projects as instructed by the supervisor. * Develop and maintain detailed knowledge of Waystone operations and the regulatory implications thereof. * Adhere to Waystone change management procedures in order to ensure efficient implementations and minimal client impact. * Assist the team in the development, implementation and monitoring of policies and procedural manuals that contribute to the efficiency of the department while also ensuring timely and accurate delivery of service and regulatory compliance. * Ensure all regulatory and compliance requirements are met for an assigned portfolio of clients. * Communicate with clients to ensure satisfaction with the service provided and to resolve any potential issues. * When instructed, independently liaise with fund service providers and fund legal counsels. To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty satisfactorily. The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. **Education** * A third level degree in Business, finance and/or related area. **Experience** * Ideally, candidates will have some prior hedge fund documentation and client service exposure or have a paralegal or corporate administrator background. * Have a strong interest in the Financial Services Sector. * Outstanding time management, organizational skills, oral/written communication skills, critical thinking and attention to detail is required. * Must be able to work in a fast\-paced environment including reporting and providing support to various members of the team. * Strong MS Office and Analytic Skills; mid\-level information management software experience required.

title: Junior Quantity Surveyor
description: **JJ Rhatigan \& Company is one of Ireland’s leading Building Contractors, with operations throughout Ireland and the UK. We are currently recruiting for a Junior Quantity Surveyor for our Dublin office.** **This is a fantastic opportunity to join a prestigious main contractor and work with like\-minded people on award winning multi\-million\-euro projects.** **Responsibilities:** * Assisting the procurement process in whatever way required. This will include but not be limited to; completing take offs, compiling tender documentation, contacting subcontractor and developing a schedule of interested companies, issuing tender enquiries and ensuring the return of quotations in line with JJR procurement schedules. * Review tender quotations and ensuring they are in line with the scope of works and drawings issued. If not, raise queries on same and ensure quotes are updated. * Compile tender comparisons on returned quotations ensuring no scope gaps have been overlooked. * Procurement of all subcontractor and consultant trades (as required) including issuing tender enquiries in line with JJR “Tender Review Checklist”, and preparing internal signoff (or parts of the signoff if requested) in line with the requirements set out in the JJR “Subcontractor appointment Checklist”. * Ensuring any trades which you have been made responsible for have been fully signed off internally and the relevant subcontractor, consultant or supplier has been issued, signed and returned a complete order in line with JJR Schedule of Subcontract Documents prior to works commencing onsite. This must be completed as it does not happen all of the time at present. This will apply to all Collateral Warranties where required. No payments will be processed until both are in place. * Assist the Commercial Lead in preparing all internal reports as outlined in JJR’s “CVR Report Requirements, Project Monthly Report, Bi\-Monthly Report and Cashflow Projection Inwards \& Outwards”, which are to be signed off and returned on the last Friday of each month depending on the Commercial Director’s requirements. * Assist in providing cost or other information to the client as requested to ensure a good working relationship is maintained. * Assist as required in compiling and agreeing monthly application for work completed by JJR to ensure a positive cashflow is maintained for each project. * Assessing Subcontractors and Consultants applications for payment on a monthly basis as requested by the commercial lead. All applications must be assessed, certificates/payless notices issued and payments uploaded to SAP for Level 1 \& 2 approval by the Senior QS and Commercial Director to allow payments to be made on time. The Subcontractor \& Consultant Payment Checklist is to be issued to the Commercial Manager once payments are loaded on SAP. * The AQS may be asked to provide assistance to the estimating department during certain periods. Tasks may include, measuring, issuing enquiries, comparing quotes etc. The AQS must be able to accurately interpret quotations and ensure sufficient coverage for JJR’s tender price. Assistance will be provided by Senior Estimators. * Maintain all project records on Workspace. This includes but is not limited to correspondence, applications (subcontractor and clients), assessment (subcontractor and client), variation registers, cost reports, cash flows, client certifications, internal costings, etc. * The AQS should carry out their works in a manner which avoids disputes, both with the client and with our supply change. If disputes do arise with the AQS must assist the commercial team in developing a defence including setting out our position, providing detailed reports, working with JJR appointed defence teams etc. * Seek value engineering options where possible to ensure JJR are achieving the best possible value for material, trades and consultants. * The AQS must be capable of working to tight deadlines. This includes assisting to ensure the following is completed on time each month, Application for Payments, Assessments of Applications, CVR Reports, Cashflows, Monthly Project Reviews etc. * The AQS will assist the Purchasing Department with the purchasing of materials in line with the Project Quantity Surveyor. This must be done in an efficient manner while obtaining the most cost\-effective solution. **Requirements:** * A third level qualification in Quantity Surveying * Ability to work as part of a team * Ability to multi\-task and work under pressure * Exceptional communication skills * Solution orientated with strong analytical and problem\-solving skills **What JJR can offer you:** * Attractive terms of Employment \& Competitive salary for the right candidate * Excellent Benefits * Excellent Career Progression * Opportunities to work on challenging projects and assignments. If you feel you have the required skills and experience and are interested in joining a highly respected company with the opportunity to progress your career, we would be interested in hearing from you.

title: Junior Quantity Surveyor
description: At JJ Rhatigan, we have been delivering excellence in construction for over 70 years. As one of Ireland's leading building contractors, we are committed to innovation, quality, sustainability, and developing talented people who want to build rewarding careers. We are currently seeking a motivated and ambitious **Junior Quantity Surveyor** to join our commercial team in Dublin. This is an excellent opportunity for a recent graduate or an early\-career Quantity Surveyor to gain hands on experience working on some of Ireland's most prestigious construction projects while learning from experienced commercial professionals. **The Role** ------------ Reporting to the Commercial Manager, Managing Quantity Surveyor, or Senior Quantity Surveyor, you will support the commercial management of projects throughout their lifecycle. You will gain exposure to procurement, subcontractor management, cost reporting, valuations, contract administration, and commercial controls while contributing to the successful delivery of major construction projects. **Key Responsibilities** ------------------------ ### **Procurement Tendering** * Assist with take\-offs and tender documentation. * Issue tender enquiries and track tender returns. * Review quotations against drawings, specifications, and scope requirements. * Prepare tender comparisons and identify commercial risks, exclusions, and scope gaps. * Support communication with subcontractors and suppliers throughout the procurement process. ### **Commercial Administration** * Assist in preparing monthly applications and valuations. * Support the assessment of subcontractor and consultant payment applications. * Assist with payment processing through SAP. * Maintain accurate commercial records and documentation. ### **Reporting Cost Control** * Support the preparation of monthly commercial reports, CVRs, cashflows, and project reviews. * Assist in monitoring project costs and forecasting financial performance. * Contribute to maintaining commercial procedures and reporting deadlines. ### **Contract Document Management** * Maintain commercial records and project documentation on SharePoint. * Support subcontractor appointment processes and ensure documentation is complete before work commences. * Assist with variation tracking and commercial correspondence. ### **Commercial Support** * Assist with value engineering initiatives and cost\-saving opportunities. * Support dispute resolution and claims preparation when required. * Provide estimating support during tender periods as needed. **What We're Looking For** -------------------------- ### **Essential** * Degree in Quantity Surveying, Construction Economics, or a related discipline. * Strong numerical and analytical skills. * Excellent attention to detail and organisational abilities. * Strong communication and interpersonal skills. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office, particularly Excel. * Ability to work effectively as part of a team and manage multiple priorities. ### **Desirable** * Previous placement, internship, or work experience within the construction industry. * Knowledge of construction contracts and commercial processes. * Experience using SAP or similar commercial systems. **What We Offer** ----------------- * Competitive salary and benefits package. * Structured career development and mentoring. * Exposure to large\-scale, high\-profile construction projects. * Ongoing professional development and training opportunities. * Support towards achieving professional accreditation. * Opportunity to grow your career with one of Ireland's leading construction companies. If you are looking to build a successful career in quantity surveying with a company that values excellence, collaboration, and professional growth, we would love to hear from you. **Apply today and become part of the JJ Rhatigan team.**

title: Junior Security Analyst, Threat Detection Operations
description: ### **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience. * 2 years of experience with security assessments or security design reviews or threat modeling. * Experience with host, network, and cloud security principles and concepts (e.g., common attacker tactics, tools, and techniques (TTPs)). ### **Preferred qualifications:** * Certification in Security\+ or similar Cyber Security/Incident Response. * Experience analyzing, triaging, and remediating common information security incidents. * Experience with automation via coding. * Ability to work separately, prioritize, and multitask. * Excellent problem\-solving, investigative, and written and verbal communication skills. * Excellent log/data analysis skill analysing a large set of data using sql or similar query language. **About the job** ----------------- In this role, you will develop and maintain the signals, tools, and infrastructure that help Google evolve to match attackers. You will perform analysis of risks on enterprise, production, and cloud environments.The Core team builds the technical foundation behind Google’s flagship products. These are the essential building blocks for excellent, safe, and coherent experiences for our users and drive the pace of innovation for every developer. As the Core team, we have a mandate and a unique opportunity to impact important technical decisions across the company.Individual pay is determined by factors including job\-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Ireland: €68000 \- €70000 (EUR) \+ 15% bonus target \+ equity \+ benefits Learn more about benefits at Google.**Responsibilities** -------------------- * Analyze, investigate, and respond to security alerts. * Research new risk hunting techniques to identify/discover security risks. * Automate workflows and develop solutions to reduce operational toil. * Ensure compliance with legal mandates and internal security and privacy policies. * Manage response efforts for low severity incidents. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. Ireland: €68000 \- €70000 (EUR) \+ 15% bonus target \+ equity \+ benefits **Responsibilities** Learn more about benefits at Google . * Analyze, investigate, and respond to security alerts. * Research new risk hunting techniques to identify/discover security risks. * Automate workflows and develop solutions to reduce operational toil. * Ensure compliance with legal mandates and internal security and privacy policies. * Manage response efforts for low severity incidents. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

title: KITCHEN TEAM MEMBER
description: We believe eating well should never mean sacrificing taste. Our kitchen team are at the heart of what we do, passionate about food, obsessed with flavour, and focused on delivering fresh, delicious dishes every day. This is a role for someone who loves being in the kitchen, has great attention to detail, and wants to grow their skills in a fast\-paced environment. **Reporting to:** General Manager **Contract:** Full\-time \& Part\-time available **Pay:** €14\.50–€15\.25 per hour depending on experience and role ****What You’ll Be Doing**** * Food Safety \& Compliance – Follow all food safety protocols and ensure everything we serve meets the highest safety standards. * Hygiene Hero – Keep the kitchen spotless and ensure equipment is always sanitised and safe to use. * Allergen Alert – Understand allergens and help ensure every dish is safely prepared for all customers. * Recipe Rundown – Work with prep sheets and help keep the kitchen running smoothly with accurate, consistent prep. * Flavour Focus – Care deeply about taste and presentation. You know great food when you see (and taste) it. * Process Perfection – Know the menu, ingredients and procedures inside out. Follow our SOPs to keep everything efficient and consistent. * Helping Hand – Support the Kitchen Leader with stock checks, rotation, and keeping an eye on kitchen operations. * All Hands on Deck – Ready to jump in when it’s busy, support the service line and work wherever you’re needed most. ****Who You Are**** * Food Enthusiast – You’re passionate about food and get genuinely excited about fresh, seasonal ingredients. * Curious – You enjoy learning new things, ask questions, and want to get better every day. * Innovative Spirit – You love being part of a team that’s always improving and evolving. * Value\-Driven – You bring a strong work ethic and a positive attitude to every shift. * Love for Hospitality – You enjoy the buzz of a restaurant and take pride in great food and great service. * Team Player – Always ready to help your teammates and support the people around you. ****Why Work at Sprout**** * Great food for you on every shift * Growth opportunities – we love to promote from within * Team discount across all locations * Well\-being and mental health support * Regular team socials and celebrations * A fun, friendly team who genuinely care ****What We’re Looking For**** You don’t need to be a chef, but you do need a passion for food and a commitment to quality. Here’s what we value most: * Love for Food – You care about flavour, freshness, and making food that looks as good as it tastes. * Knife Skills – You know your way around a chopping board (or you’re eager to learn). Perfectly sliced carrots? No problem. * Attention to Detail – From portion sizes to plating, you notice the little things that make a big difference. * Good Level of English – You can follow instructions, communicate with your team, and understand food safety requirements. * Reliable \& Organised – You keep things clean, follow checklists, and stay on top of your prep. * Curious \& Willing to Learn – You’re excited to build your skills and grow with the team. If you’re someone who thrives in a team, loves a bit of buzz, and wants to grow in a company that values people and positivity, we’d love to hear from you.

title: L3 Infrastructure Engineer
description: This isn't a heads\-down ticket\-closing role. We need someone who can run complex infrastructure projects end\-to\-end, manage major incidents with calm authority, and explain technical decisions clearly to a non\-technical senior leadership team. If you've been the person your team turns to when things get hard, this is the role for you. **What you'll own** * **Change management** Own the full CAB process. Coordinate vendors and stakeholders, present changes in plain English to a non\-technical SMT, and produce clear post\-change documentation every time. * **P1 / major incident management** Lead major incidents from detection to resolution. Chase vendors, keep leadership informed at every step, and write up root cause analysis reports to close out properly * **Infrastructure project delivery** Take projects from design to go\-live. You won't be handed a single piece of someone else's plan — you'll own the whole thing and coordinate everyone around it * **Platform ownership** VMware vSphere (independent builds and upgrades), Veeam backup and DR, Windows Server 2016–2022, Azure / M365, and firewall management across the estate **What we're looking for** * 4\+ years in a corporate infrastructure environment with demonstrable project leadership * Hands\-on VMware vSphere: not just certification awareness, real build and upgrade ownership * Veeam backup and DR: configured and managed independently * Strong Windows Server (2016–2022\), Azure / M365, and firewall management experience * Compliance framework awareness: ISO 27001, SOX, NIST * ITIL\-aligned thinking: especially around change and incident management * Clear, confident communicator who can talk to both a NOC engineer and a CFO without changing what they're saying, just how they say it * Financial services or MSP background

title: Life & Pensions Administrator
description: **Company Overview** Walfrid Private is a growing Wealth Management firm based in Ballymount, Dublin 12\. We partner with Irish Company Directors, Business Owners and Entrepreneurs to help them reduce costs, reduce taxes and build long\-term financial security. Our business is built on high\-quality advice, strong planning and crucially, excellent execution. As we continue to grow nationwide, the strength of our Broker Support function is more important than ever in ensuring every client strategy is delivered smoothly, accurately and on time. **Role Overview** We are currently seeking an energetic and detail\-driven Life \& Pensions Administrator to join our Broker Support team. This role sits at the heart of how advice is turned into action and offers the chance to work closely with experienced Financial Advisors in a fast\-moving, professional wealth management environment. You will be involved in the full lifecycle of pension, investment and protection business \- from application and setup through to ongoing administration \- ensuring cases progress efficiently and to a high standard. While the role is primarily administration\-focused, it is varied, busy and hands\-on, ideal for someone who enjoys momentum, structure and being part of a team that gets things done. **Key Responsibilities** * Process pension, investment and protection business submitted by the Financial Advisory team * Manage and track new applications across life company portals * Set up and maintain client and policy records on the CRM system * Prepare and issue compliance documentation alongside Financial Advisors, including statements of suitability and supporting reports * Ensure files are accurate, complete and compliant with internal processes and regulatory requirements * Liaise with life companies and internal teams to progress cases and resolve queries efficiently * Handle policy servicing requests such as amendments, withdrawals and encashments * Manage daily post and correspondence and keep cases moving * Prepare, maintain and update client and policy files * Provide a high standard of service via telephone and email * Support cross\-training within the team to ensure strong operational support across the business **Skills \& Experience Required** * Minimum of 1 years’ experience working with pension and protection products in a brokerage, life company or similar environment * QFA qualification completed or currently in progress * Solid understanding of Life, Pension and Investment products in the Irish broker market * Comfortable working within regulated processes and compliance frameworks * Strong organisational skills and attention to detail * Positive, proactive approach to work and teamwork

title: MEP Detailer – Dublin
description: * Permanent position available with a considerable portfolio of works for the next 5\+ years * To build your career by assisting in the delivery of ‘leading edge’ engineering projects. * To work with a vibrant, agile and multi\-functional team in delivering projects on time, safely, to budget and to the required quality standards. * To benefit from Kirby’s Career Development Programmes that will enhance your leadership capability. * To work with the best. * Excellent package on offer with room for negotiations **About Kirby Group Engineering** Kirby Group Engineering is an international multi\-disciplinary engineering services contractor and leading provider of high\-value engineering services to a number of ‘blue chip’ companies. Operating across Ireland, the UK and Europe, Kirby has become the engineering service provider of choice in growth segments such as pharmaceuticals, data centres and power transmission and distribution. Our culture is innovative, collaborative and performance focused. The successful candidate will have the opportunity to grow and develop in a company committed to developing talent and rewarding performance. **Role Purpose** Reporting to Lead MEP Coordinator the MEP Detailer has responsibility for delivering all 3D and 2D co\-ordination milestones on each project. The role of the MEP Detailer is project specific and all engineers to be based on site within a BIM environment when requested. When not working on a specific project the MEP Detailer will be based within their Business Unit office and report to the Lead MEP Coordinator. They may need to assist with other smaller projects and produce tender information. **Key Functions Of The MEP Detailer** * Support the project team with regard to all co\-ordination deliverables * Delivering all 3D and 2D deliverables set by the BIM lead * Assisting the Lead MEP Coordinator with production of 2D co\-ordination and installation drawings * Assisting the Lead MEP Coordinator with 3D elements needed for design and co\-ordination * Supporting all parties working within the BIM process * Working within a team environment help to achieve efficient project delivery **MEP Detailer Responsibilities** In conjunction with your project team, you will be responsible for the execution of various stages of a project as follows: **Project Delivery** * Detailing of 3D elements needed for design and co\-ordination * Production of 3D model files through Revit and where needed Fabrication Cad * Production of 2D co\-ordination and installation drawings * Ensuring that detailed model files are co\-ordinated and clash free by clashing against federated model on a constant basis (every 30min) * Taking direct instructions from Lead MEP Detailer on all design issues * Administration of Vault files * Clash detection of submitted files * Daily building of federated model * Ensure all files submitted in correct format * Attend weekly co\-ordination meetings when requested * Support all parties working within the BIM process * Assist with clash resolutions on site * Verification and preparation of layout drawings to meet contract requirements * Confers with project engineer and project management staff to resolve problems and explain drawings to production or construction teams and providing adjustments as necessary * Production of all verified record drawings * Site surveying \& verification including point cloud scanning and total station operation * Production of all 2D project drawings * Updating and administrating of all co\-ordination documents registers and drawings * Assist with tenders and other drawing requirements from the business unit. **BIM Development** * Work within a designated project working as part of a project team * Gain knowledge of mechanical \& electrical systems with regard to 3D co\-ordination * Develop knowledge of onsite co\-ordination, fabrication and construction * Attend co\-ordination meetings and develop communication skills * Move to a Lead MEP Coordinator position when the opportunity arises and deemed ready by the BIM manger **Necessary requirement of the MEP Detailer** * 3rd level qualification in Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Building Services Engineering or Strong trades background coupled with a BIM qualification * A minimum of 3\+ years’ experience within a BIMCo\-ordination function * Mechanical or Electrical background and have a proven track record in a similar role * Proven ability to lead a team * Strong communication and organisational skills * Ability to work under pressure and to short deadlines **Desired Competencies / Special Role Requirements** ***For more information on this Project BIM Engineer/ BIM Coordinator position or other BIM Engineer / BIM Coordinator opportunities based in Ireland the UK or Europe contact Kirby Group Talent Acquisitions Team confidentially on \+353 (0\)1 454 0411 or apply in confidence.***

title: MERCHANDISER - ROI
description: LEARN MORE ABOUT US AND THE ROLE:**Location:** Knocklyon, Dublin (D16 P6EK, A96 P2R9\) **Hours:** 2 hours per week **Days:** Monday **Benefits:** Holiday Entitlement, Merchandiser Recognition Programme, Employee Benefits Scheme, Expenses/Mileage/Travel Time where applicable As an inclusive employer Carlton Greetings are passionate about creating a workforce that represents the diversity of our communities and the customers we supply. We encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply for this flexible and customer focused role where every day offers fresh challenges; from recognising new sales/merchandising opportunities, to displaying brand new ranges. As a part time Greeting Card Merchandiser you will be the face of Carlton Greetings, calling upon retail outlets in and around your local area, ensuring our Customers’ displays are of the highest standard. **The role involves:** * Visiting local retailers, high street chains or supermarkets * Tidying \& filling greeting card \& gift dressing displays * Locating \& unpacking deliveries for replenishment of displays * Installation of new displays * No cold calling or direct selling is involved **To be a Part Time Merchandiser, you will need to have:** * Reliable \& conscientious work ethic * Flexibility around working times \& notably during peak seasonal periods when overtime is likely to be required * The initiative \& ability to work on your own * A strong team ethic * A confident \& friendly personality * No experience is required, as we offer full training * Full driving licence (desirable) So if you love to get out and about, enjoy meeting new people and would like to be part of a great team that promotes and values diversity, then this may be the role for you! WHO WE ARE At Carlton Greetings we are passionate about creating an inclusive workplace that promotes and values diversity. We help make the world a more thoughtful and caring place every single day with our greeting cards, gift dressings and social expressions products created for life’s big events, special occasions and those “saw\-this\-and\-thought\-of\-you” moments of life. Along with our sister Company John Sands, we’re part of the American Greetings Corporation, one of the largest privately owned greeting card publishers in the world. We’re one of the largest direct to retail publisher of greeting cards and social expression products in the UK \- so you’ll find our amazing products just about everywhere! **Here at Carlton Greetings we take pride in being an inclusive and diverse employer \- we** **are dedicated and committed to building a culture where difference is valued and** **everyone is able to** **fulfil** **their potential at work regardless of their background.**

title: MHPSS Cultural Mediation Associate (Georgian)
description: Applications are welcome from internal and external candidates. For all IOM vacancies, applications from qualified and eligible first\-tier candidates are considered before those of qualified and eligible second\-tier candidates in the selection process. For the purpose of this vacancy, internal candidates are considered first\-tier candidates. Context IOM Ireland has been operating in Ireland since 2001, currently in following areas of migration management: Assisted Voluntary Return and Reintegration, Resettlement, Family Reunification, Counter\-Trafficking, Protection, Integration Support to Beneficiaries of Temporary Protection, Integrated Immigration Governance and Mental Health and Psychosocial Support (MHPSS). IOM provides mental health and psychosocial support to international protection seekers residing in the Citywest accommodation facility with the aim to improve their psychosocial well\-being and to strengthen their overall resilience. Under the overall supervision of the Chief of Mission in Ireland, the Head of MHPSS Unit, the Cultural Mediation and Community Engagement Coordinator and direct supervision of Senior Cultural Mediation Associate, the incumbent will support the implementation of activities related to community mobilization and pertinent activities for international protection applicants hosted in Citywest accommodation centre. * RESPONSIBILITIES Provide cultural mediation and interpretation support by facilitating communication between service providers and migrants (from English into different migrants’ mother tongue and vice versa), while always respecting the principles of neutrality, accuracy, impartiality, confidentiality. * Provide verbal interpretation support to the International Protection Accommodation Services (IPAS) staff in Citywest to support day\-to\-day communication with beneficiaries from English into different migrants’ languages. * Assist through interpretation during the collection of routine information and data and case identification and referrals. * Support the beneficiaries in contacting service providers within Citywest by providing interpretation and mediation support in an impartial and neutral manner. * Support the identification of vulnerabilities from the initial reception into the centre and throughout the continued communication with beneficiaries and refer them to the Senior Case Management Associate for case management. * Support IOM partners in the implementation of community engagement and awareness\-raising activities by assisting with logistics, interpretation, and beneficiary outreach. * Conduct outreach activities to inform beneficiaries about available MHPSS services and scheduled activities, in coordination with MHPSS team. * Provide interpretation assistance to mental health professionals during scheduled group and individual counselling sessions, focus group discussions and psycho\-education activities, including remote interpretation (phone or online), when required. * Prepare routine documentation related to the project and submit basic reports to the Project Management Team on activities performed in Citywest. * Any other related task, as required. QUALIFICATIONS Education * High school diploma with five years of relevant experience; or, * Bachelor’s degree in Communication, Social Sciences, International relations or related fields from an accredited academic institution with at least 3 years of relevant professional experience. Accredited Universities are those listed in the UNESCO World Higher Education Database . Experience * Completion of trainings in cultural mediation for mental health settings, and competence in the MHPSS specialized vocabulary would be an asset. * Experience in cultural mediation in the framework of migrants’ assistance. * Previous experience with international organizations, NGOs, EU Agencies and/or Irish law enforcements or Government’s Departments would be an asset. Skills * Ability to work with migrants and asylum seekers. * Interpersonal skills (i.e. empathy and emotional resilience) * Intercultural competence and communication skills. * Culture\-specific knowledge. * Capacity to prevent and mitigate misunderstandings due to language barriers by facilitating communication in an objective and impartial manner. * Basic understanding in interpersonal communication and sensitivity in addressing the asylum\-seekers’ claims. * Knowledge of the procedures related to the asylum application in Ireland. * Ability to prepare clear and concise routine reports and basic written summaries. * Good working knowledge of Microsoft Office. Languages * For this position, fluency in English is required (oral and written). * Fluency or mother tongue in Georgian is required IOM’s official languages are English, French and Spanish. Proficiency of language(s) required will be specifically evaluated during the selection process, which may include written and/or oral assessments. **Required Competencies** Competencies will be assessed during the selection process. Values \- all IOM staff members must abide by and demonstrate these three values: * Inclusion and respect for diversity: Respects and promotes individual and cultural differences. Encourages diversity and inclusion. * Integrity and transparency: Maintains high ethical standards and acts in a manner consistent with organizational principles/rules and standards of conduct. * Professionalism: Demonstrates ability to work in a composed, competent and committed manner and exercises careful judgment in meeting day\-to\-day challenges. * Courage: Demonstrates willingness to take a stand on issues of importance. * Empathy: Shows compassion for others, makes people feel safe, respected and fairly treated. Core Competencies – behavioural indicators (Level 1\) * Teamwork: Develops and promotes effective collaboration within and across units to achieve shared goals and optimize results. * Delivering results: Produces and delivers quality results in a service\-oriented and timely manner. Is action oriented and committed to achieving agreed outcomes. * Managing and sharing knowledge: Continuously seeks to learn, share knowledge and innovate. * Accountability: Takes ownership for achieving the Organization’s priorities and assumes responsibility for own actions and delegated work. * Communication: Encourages and contributes to clear and open communication. Explains complex matters in an informative, inspiring and motivational way. Managerial Competencies – behavioural indicators (Level 1\) (applicable only if position is with direct reports) * Leadership: Provides a clear sense of direction, leads by example and demonstrates the ability to carry out the Organization’s vision. Assists others to realize and develop their leadership and professional potential. * Empowering others: Creates an enabling environment where staff can contribute their best and develop their potential. * Building Trust: Promotes shared values and creates an atmosphere of trust and honesty. * Strategic thinking and vision: Works strategically to realize the Organization’s goals and communicates a clear strategic direction. * Humility: Leads with humility and shows openness to acknowledging own shortcomings. **Notes*** Any offer made to the candidate in relation to this vacancy notice is subject to funding confirmation. * This selection process may be used to staff similar positions in various duty stations. Recommended candidates will remain eligible to be appointed in a similar position for a period of 24 months. * This post is subject to local recruitment. Only those holding a valid residence and work permit for the country where this position is based will be eligible for consideration. * Appointment will be subject to certification that the candidate is medically fit for appointment, verification of residency, visa, and authorizations by the concerned Government, where applicable. * IOM has a zero\-tolerance policy on conduct that is incompatible with the aims and objectives of the United Nations and IOM, including sexual exploitation and abuse, sexual harassment, abuse of authority and discrimination based on gender, nationality, age, race, sexual orientation, religious or ethnic background or disabilities. * IOM does not charge a fee at any stage of its recruitment process (application, interview, processing, training or other fee). IOM does not request any information related to bank accounts. * IOM only accepts duly completed applications submitted through the IOM online recruitment system. The online tool also allows candidates to track the status of their application. * A s the right to live and work in Ireland is required, the contract issued may be shorter than the period mentioned in the advertisement, in order to align with expiry date of the work permit. For further information and other job postings, you are welcome to visit our website: IOM Careers and Job Vacancies

title: MSAT Oral Solid Drug Product Expert CMVD Monotherapy
description: Date de parution: 23 avr. 2026 Ville: Arklow, County Wicklow, Ireland Pays/Région: Ireland Type de contrat: Permanent Contract N° offre: 10664 **MSAT Oral Solid Drug Product Expert CMVD Monotherapy (France, Ireland or Poland)** We are a human\-scale, international, and independent pharmaceutical group governed by a Foundation. Our unique model makes us proud and, more importantly, enables us to fully serve our mission: "committed to therapeutic progress for the benefit of patients." In R\&D, we also focus on creating new medicines that slow down or halt the progression of rare neurological disorders and significantly improve patient quality of life. Who are we? 22,000 passionate individuals from over 50 nationalities, driven by an entrepreneurial spirit. Every day, we move forward with and for patients, with and for our teams, motivated by the desire to care, to dare, to grow, and to commit to being useful to those in need. Come and experience and contribute to our commitment \#MovedByYou. www.servier.com **Within Servier Industry, the Manufacturing Science and Analytical Technology (MSAT) organization plays a key role in driving technical excellence, industrial transformation, and lifecycle management — enabling manufacturing robustness, quality, and compliance across all product modalities and technologies.** **Location: Arklow, County Wicklow, Ireland** **Role Summary** Within MSAT, the Oral Solid Drug Product team is responsible for providing technical stewardship and manufacturing science support across oral solid drug product lifecycle. The Oral Solid Drug Product Expert serves as the technical subject matter expert for CMVD monotherapies reporting to MSAT Oral Solid Drug Product CMVD (Cardio Metabo Venous Diseases) Monotherapy Lead. This role provides scientific and process expertise to support the manufacturing and lifecycle management of CMVD oral solid drug products across internal and external manufacturing sites. **Primary Responsibilities** Technical Oversight of Internal and External Manufacturing Processes * Act as the technical process owner for CMVD monotherapy products within MSAT scope at internal and external manufacturing sites. * Provide technical oversight of manufacturing processes for assigned oral solid commercial products at Servier internal sites in collaboration with Site MSAT and at external partners (CMOs) as part of Virtual Plant Teams * Lead internal cross\-functional Technical Teams to facilitate drug product related technical topics in collaboration with other functions * Prepare and manage meeting agendas, minutes, and action follow\-up * Facilitate alignment on technical project timelines, supply impact, and budget considerations. * Lead or support deviation investigations, root cause analyses, and CAPA definition for process related issues, ensuring robust closure and knowledge transfer. * Maintain and oversee process monitoring and Continued Process Verification (CPV) for assigned commercial products across manufacturing sites. * Build and maintain MSAT knowledge repository for assigned commercial products and processes Technology Transfer and Lifecycle Management * Lead or support tech transfers, scale changes, PPQ, and launch readiness activities across global sites. * Conduct and document process fit assessments, risk evaluations, and FMEA as part of the lifecycle management strategy. * Participate in process optimization efforts, including yield, throughput, and quality performance improvements across the network. CMC and Regulatory Support * Author or review CMC\-related sections of regulatory submissions (e.g., post\-approval changes, variations, new market filings as needed). * Support Regulatory CMC in responding to health authority questions related to commercial manufacturing processes and controls. * Ensure manufacturing and validation strategies are aligned with GMP, ICH, and global regulatory expectations. Subject Matter Expertise and Continuous Improvement * Support implementation and optimization of manufacturing processes and related control/sampling strategies (e.g., granulations, compression, coating, pellet layering, capsule filling, segregation control measures). * Specify and support qualification/optimization of tooling and equipment such as: tablet presses, precision capsule fillers for pellets, fluid\-bed coaters, high\-shear granulators, roller compactors, and containment/segregation equipment. * Anticipate scale\-dependent challenges (e.g., compression dwell time effects, layer adhesion, segregation during handling) and define scale\-up acceptance criteria, equivalence studies and narrative for regulatory dossiers. * Recommend and deploy PAT tools where beneficial (NIR, weight/force monitoring, in\-line content estimation) for real\-time or near\-real\-time assurance of processes * Perform risk and criticality assessments to identify gaps and define improvements in line with quality, regulatory, and business expectations. * Stay current with industry trends, scientific literature, and evolving technologies related to OSD manufacturing and MSAT best practices **Education And Required Skills** * Minimum Bachelor’s Degree in Pharmaceutical Sciences or Chemical Engineering or related technical field with minimum of 8\-10 years relevant experience, or preferably a Masters or PhD in Pharmaceutical Sciences or Chemical Engineering or related technical field with a minimum of 5\-8 years of relevant experience in drug product formulation and process development and manufacturing support including experience with regulatory submissions * In\-depth knowledge of formulation concepts/techniques across immediate\-release and extendedrelease oral solid dosage forms and relevant manufacturing processes, e.g., blending, granulation including dry/roller compaction and wet/high\-shear/fluid bed technologies, tablet compression, tablet coating, capsule filling etc * In\-depth knowledge of primary container closure components used for oral solid dosage forms * Expertise in use/application of basic statistical methodologies and softwares (e.g., Minitab, JMP or equivalent) * Expertise in use/application of variety of problem\-solving tools e.g. Ishikawa, Kepner\-Tregoe (KT), five\-whys, etc. * Experience leading or supporting tech transfers, deviation investigations, change controls, and regulatory documentation. * Strong knowledge of GMP, ICH guidelines, and regulatory submission expectations for commercial products. * Comfortable working in a global matrixed team with cross\-functional collaboration across Quality, Manufacturing, Supply Chain, and Regulatory. * Strong ability to strategically plan, organize, and manage multiple projects simultaneously * Excellent written/oral communication and presentation skills * Excellent communication and documentation skills; able to interface effectively with internal stakeholders and external partners. * A continuous improvement and learning mindset, aligned with Servier’s strategic direction and patient\-first values. We value both experience and the desire to engage daily in contributing to therapeutic progress for the benefit of patients. If this offer resonates with you, seize this opportunity to meet us!

title: Market Data Business Analyst
description: **Overview** **What we do** We are experts in trading essentially all listed financial products and asset classes, with a focus on derivatives trading. Through market making and market taking, we handle millions of trading transactions around the world every day, providing liquidity and ensuring competitive prices for buyers and sellers. While our presence in the market is broad, our trading desks are highly specialized, allowing for a deep understanding of unique drivers of each asset class. **Job Summary** We are currently seeking a Market Data Business Analyst to work in a growing, diverse and challenging real\-time market data environment. The successful candidate will provide expertise in vendor relationship management, cost management, market data compliance and analysis and change management. They will lead projects related to Market Data governance through the business to successful conclusions and ensure best practice policy and procedure is being followed at all times. The candidate will represent Susquehanna in meetings of Market Data user groups such as iPug and ensure Susquehanna is utilising all available industry standards and knowledge to minimise exposure to Market Data costs. It is essential that the candidate has a proven track record working in the financial services industry specifically in the Enterprise Market Data domain. They should have experience with Market Data entitlements system(s) e.g. DACS or DART, dealing with exchanges and vendors in the market data space. The successful candidate will be able to analyse the existing cost base for Market Data spend and look for opportunities to reduce it by sourcing feeds from a variety of providers. The candidate must have extensive knowledge and experience of exchange specific data feeds, and be able to understand and map this to business specific data content and performance requirements. The candidate should also have a good understanding of the trading environment, rules and methodology in use by the major exchanges across Europe and other major global exchanges. They will work as part of a team to respond to audits carried out by Exchanges or 3rd parties, report to senior managers on the outcomes and take any necessary actions resulting from them. The role will involve working closely with market data engineering, infrastructure engineering teams, application developers, and business users to proactively enhance the market data stability and optimise business performance. The successful candidate will work with colleagues in the US and Sydney offices to ensure homogenisation of global policies and practices where these are possible. The candidate will liaise regularly with the business and technology teams to provide market data subject matter expertise. Good interpersonal, communication and task management skills are a must and the candidate must be able to work off his/her own initiative to ensure the market data objectives of the business are met to an agreed expected standard. **Responsibilities Include** * Liaise with business and technology users to understand current and desired market data usage requirements. This may often involve seeking and establishing new business relationships. In these cases, liaise with the business user and relevant internal legal and technology teams to confirm the suitability of the data and intended provisioning, and thereafter to coordinate data procurement. * Proactively analyse market data announcements, assessing the relevance and impact to our current market data production environment, highlighting impact to the relevant technology and business teams. * Market Data Cost Control Management specifically driving market data cost efficiency initiatives and cost management reporting to senior Management. * Market Data Exchange Audit Management * Internal Susquehanna Trading Application Market Data Controls Audit Management * Vendor Management and point of escalation for Internal Susquehanna Market Data customers. * Manage on\-going relationships with vendors, to understand impact and prerequisites of Exchange driven changes, work with pre\-sales on new Enterprise solutions, and escalate any production or cost impacting issues * Build strong relationship with application teams and business leads to understand fine detail around normalized Enterprise data requirements and supply required enhancements where possible * Development of inventory, financial and contractual controls for all market data services including report generation for exchanges and management **What We're Looking For** * 8\+ years’ experience in financial markets and applications. Exposure to working with real time market data exchange feeds in a low latency environment is an advantage * Strong Market Data Experience and Domain knowledge is essential. * Experience in NYSE Technologies market data platform, Bloomberg (terminals, Data License, B\-pipe) and Refinitive RMDS software concepts, configuration and understanding of their inner workings * Enthusiastic, hardworking, and detail oriented, with the ability to think creatively and to communicate with confidence and clarity at all levels * Exposure to Project Management is an advantage. *If you're a recruiting agency and want to partner with us, please reach out to recruiting@sig.com. Any resume or referral submitted in the absence of a signed agreement will not be eligible for an agency fee.*
