Our programmes focus on looking for early signs of disease in healthy people, so that we can: * detect health issues early * help prevent disease * ensure that anyone who does develop the disease being screened for has the best chance of early, more effective treatment * reduce people’s risk of developing a disease or its complications * support people in making informed decisions about their health **Choose Screening:** Together we can make a difference the National Screening Service 5year Strategic Plan outlining our strategic priorities and goals for the years 2023\-2027\. **Our mission:** We deliver population screening programmes that help prevent, reduce the risk of, and assist the recognition of, disease in Ireland. **Our vision:** To work together to save lives and improve people’s health through population screening. The role of the National Screening Service also includes policy, development and advice. **The Opportunity** To assist and support the delivery of client care under the supervision and direction of Radiography Service Manager or designate. The Radiography Assistant post at BreastCheck is broad based and includes assisting in Assessment Clinics and providing support to the Clinical Team to clients who have or fear they may have breast cancer. There is currently **one permanent and wholetime vacancy** available in BreastCheck Eccles Screening Unit, 36 Eccles Street, Dublin 1 A panel will be formed for future permanent and specified purpose vacancies in BreastCheck Merrion Screening Unit, Merrion Road, Dublin 4 **Principal Duties And Responsibilities** * The primary role of the Radiography Assistant is to assist, Radiographers \& Radiologists with the general day to day management of client care and associated duties in BreastCheck. * To deliver a quality, safe, efficient and caring radiology and imaging service to clients under the supervision of the Radiography Service Manager or designate. * To assist radiographers, radiologists and the multi\-disciplinary team with all duties pertaining to breast cancer screening, assessment and administration. * The Radiography Assistant is broad based and includes assisting in Assessment Clinics and providing support to the clinical team to clients who have or fear they may have breast cancer. **Please Refer To Job Description For Detailed Eligibilty Criteria** Join our team and we'll provide you with the support you need to deliver and succeed. This is an unmissable opportunity to join a diverse and supportive workplace where staff feel valued. \#ChooseScreening

title: Network Support Engineer
description: Our team is building resilient, secure, and scalable cloud platforms that support the next generation of digital financial applications. We’re looking for a Platform Engineer with hands\-on experience in AWS infrastructure and networks to help build and support our cloud\-native environments. In this role, you will work as a key contributor within our cloud platform team, focusing on automation, infrastructure as code, and secure delivery pipelines. You’ll collaborate with engineering, security, and operations teams to deliver well\-architected solutions aligned with industry best practices. • 7\+ years of experience in software engineering, platform engineering, or site reliability engineering, including 5\+ years of direct experience with AWS. • Proven experience designing production\-grade cloud infrastructure in regulated environments. • Deep understanding of cloud security, networking, identity and access management, and governance within AWS. • Advanced knowledge of networking (firewalls, DNS, Load Balancing, Proxies, etc.) • Proficiency in a scripting or programming language (e.g., Python, Go, Shell). • Experience with continuous integration/delivery pipelines and GitOps workflows. • Excellent communication skills and a collaborative mindset. • Experience with multi\-account cloud architecture, landing zones, or control tower setups. • Familiarity with zero\-trust architecture, data encryption, and key management systems (KMS). • Strong proficiency with using Infrastructure as Code tools such as Terraform to build cloud infrastructure solutions and automating cloud deployments. • Prior experience in financial services, fintech, or other regulated industries. • Hands\-on experience with containerization (Docker) and orchestration platforms (e.g., EKS/Kubernetes). • Experience with monitoring tools such as Grafana, Prometheus, Splunk, or Dynatrace, as well as AWS native tools • Experience optimizing cost and performance across large\-scale cloud environments. • Contributions to open\-source projects or active participation in cloud engineering communities.

title: New Product Development Specialist
description: **Company** Our client is an established Irish food ingredients business supplying dry and functional ingredient solutions across meat, bakery, snacking, savoury, and broader food manufacturing applications. The company operates with a strong technical and customer support focus, partnering with leading international ingredient manufacturers to deliver customised ingredient systems and application solutions. The business has an established position within the Irish market alongside growing international activity. It places significant emphasis on innovation, technical expertise, food safety, and sustainable product development aligned with evolving customer, regulatory, and environmental requirements. **The Opportunity** The NPD \& Applications Technologist will play a key role in driving innovation, concept development and customer\-led product solutions across a broad range of food categories including meat, protein, bakery, savoury and snacking applications. Reporting to the Head of Business Development, the role combines culinary creativity, food science, ingredient functionality and customer engagement. The successful candidate will be responsible for interpreting market trends, developing innovative product concepts, showcasing ingredient solutions through relevant food applications, and supporting the commercialisation of new ideas. Working closely with customers, suppliers and internal teams, the successful candidate will help develop new product ideas and bring them from concept stage through to commercial launch. This position provides exposure to leading global ingredient manufacturers, emerging food trends and a wide variety of applications across the food manufacturing sector. **Responsibilities** **Innovation \& Concept Development** * Interpret market, consumer and category trends to identify new product opportunities * Develop innovative food concepts aligned with customer requirements and business growth objectives * Create and showcase ingredient solutions across meat, protein, bakery, savoury and snacking applications * Develop kitchen\-scale recipes and concepts for further technical development and commercialisation * Translate market trends and customer needs into relevant product concepts and development projects **Customer Engagement \& Applications** * Support customer innovation projects through concept development, product demonstrations and technical presentations * Participate in customer meetings, presentations and application showcases * Work closely with customers to understand market requirements and develop tailored ingredient solutions * Demonstrate how ingredients perform across different food applications and manufacturing environments **Product Development \& Commercialisation** * Support new product development and commercial launch activities * Conduct kitchen, pilot and factory trials where required to validate product performance and scalability * Reformulate and optimise products based on customer, technical and commercial requirements * Work closely with technical, commercial and operations teams to bring new products from concept through to launch **Supplier \& Market Collaboration** * Build relationships with ingredient suppliers and utilise supplier innovation to strengthen product pipelines * Participate in supplier meetings, training sessions and innovation workshops * Maintain awareness of ingredient technologies, market developments and emerging consumer trends **Documentation \& Compliance** * Prepare technical documentation, recipes, specifications and application notes * Ensure compliance with food safety, quality and regulatory requirements * Maintain accurate project records and development documentation **Candidate Requirements** * Chef background combined with a qualification in Food Science, Food Technology, Culinary Arts or a related discipline * 3\+ years’ experience within NPD, Innovation, Applications or Product Development roles, ideally within food ingredients, processed meat, value\-added foods or related sectors * Strong interest in food innovation and new product development * Experience developing, matching and showcasing flavour profiles across a range of food applications * Strong understanding of ingredient functionality and food formulation * Experience presenting concepts, products and ideas to customers or internal stakeholders * Experience conducting kitchen, pilot and factory trials * Good understanding of customer requirements and the ability to develop practical product solutions * Knowledge of food safety systems including HACCP, BRC and relevant food industry standards * Excellent communication, organisational and project management skills * Ability to operate independently while collaborating effectively across technical, commercial and operational teams * Enthusiastic, self\-motivated and genuinely interested in food and product development * Comfortable presenting products and concepts to customers **Remuneration** An attractive remuneration package is on offer depending on experience. For candidates bringing particularly strong technical depth, relevant applications experience, and sector knowledge, there is flexibility within the package depending on overall fit. If you’re interested, please don’t hesitate to reach out to us at jobs@consultghi.com for further detail. To apply, kindly submit your CV and cover letter in Word format, outlining your qualifications and relevant experience.

title: Night Shift Operator
description: **Night Shift Operator \- Applegreen Carlow Killeshin Road** As a Night Shift Operator at Applegreen, you will be a crucial part of our front\-line operations. If you’re looking for a fulfilling role where you can make a difference every day and build lasting relationships, we’d love to hear from you! Apply now to join the Applegreen family and become part of a company that values community, teamwork, our customers. * Experience in a similar role is an advantage **What will I be doing as a Night Shift Operator at Applegreen?** * Operating the shop in line with company policy throughout the night as per shop checklist. * Ensure all perimeter alarms are activated. * Ensure the site building is secure throughout the night. * Keep the shop area clean, tidy, and safe. * Provide excellent customer service. * Ensure accurate price labelling of products. * Stock rotation and shelf stacking. **Why Should I join the Applegreen Team?** **Benefits** * All colleagues are eligible for a discount, allowing exclusive discounts on Bakewell Deli foods and hot drinks. * Bike to work scheme (available after 6 months of service). * HSF Health Plans schemes for healthcare expenses including dental, optical and many more. * Wellbeing platform with micro\-modules and articles to support your mental health and wellbeing. In addition, our Employee Assistance Programme is a free confidential counselling service which offers support on personal, family, work, and money matters. * Flexible schedules. * Company pension scheme. * Exclusive offers on broadband and mobile plans. * Refer a friend scheme. * Development opportunities through a variety of online and classroom\-based learning delivered by inhouse and external industry experts. **An Applegreen Night Shift Operator would ideally:** * Have previous experience in retail * Have a can\-do attitude who has strong communication skills and enjoys interacting with customers. * Ability to work well with colleagues, contributing to a positive team environment. * Enjoy working in a fast\-paced environment. * Willingness to learn and adapt to new tasks.

title: OTC Sales Assistant
description: OTC Sales Assistant\- Woodstown **Our people make the difference.** Here at PHX Ireland, we strive to be a place for everyone where each and every one of us can be our true, authentic selves, every single day. For us, individuality sparks new ideas, shapes new approaches and brings us powerful new perspectives. It’s the heartbeat of our innovation, creativity, and success. Which is why, at PHX Ireland, we don’t just embrace our differences – we celebrate them. Across United Drug, McCabes Pharmacy, and TCP Homecare, in every location and every role, the way we work makes us who we are – and that’s something we are really proud of. Which is why we’ve summed up our ethos in our six PHX Ireland values; Customer focused, Quality driven, collaborative, ambitious, innovative, and inclusive. We currently have an exciting opportunity for an **OTC Sales Assistant** to join our team in Woodstown. We have a **part\-time, permanent** contract available. ***It isn’t just any shop floor, it’s the heart of the community.*** **Why You'll Love Working With Us** 💼 We’ve got your back: employee assistance programme 🛍️ Perks you’ll actually use: Generous in store discounts 🌈 Belonging at Work: Inclusive, welcoming team culture where you’re valued for *you* . 👶 Family comes first: Life Stage Policies including maternity and paternity pay **What You’ll Be Doing:** * Drive Sales: increase O.T.C sales through link selling. * Put Patients First: advise and assist customers with all store products. * Deliver Exceptional Patient Care: engage actively with customers and provide a high standard in customer service. * Stay Fully Informed: develop excellent product knowledge. * Point of Sale Accuracy: Perform till duties with efficiency, accuracy, and attention to detail, **About McCabes Pharmacy** McCabes Pharmacy is one of Ireland's most loved Pharmacy providers, pioneering truly innovative services over the past 40 years. We are founded on values of compassionate care, advice you can trust, speedy service and excellent value for money. The patient and customer are truly at the heart of what we do. The teams that serve our customers are central to our organisation. Our commitment to our staff is to enable them to develop their careers and to have as many opportunities to be the best they can be. **About PHX Ireland \& PHOENIX Group** We are comprised of United Drug, Ireland’s leading pharmaceutical wholesaler and distributor; McCabes Pharmacy, Ireland’s leading community pharmacy chain; and TCP Homecare, innovators in clinical home nursing services and direct to patient delivery solutions. PHX Ireland Group employs over 1,800 people, operates 5 distribution centres and pharmacies nationwide, delivering over 85 million packs to Irish pharmacies and hospitals and serving 500,000 patients each year. PHX Ireland is part of the PHOENIX group, Europe’s leading healthcare provider. Headquartered in Mannheim, Germany, the PHOENIX group brings together more than 48,000 employees from 29 countries, includes 223 pharmaceutical distribution sites, and more than 3200 owned pharmacies and 17000 partner pharmacies. ***We strive to foster an open and inclusive environment, embracing applications from individuals of all backgrounds. Our commitment lies in ensuring an accessible interview process for all candidates. If you require additional support or accommodations to attend an interview, we kindly encourage you to reach out to*** ***jobs@mccabespharmacy.ie***

title: Office Administrator
description: We’ve worked hard to build a culture where people feel a genuine sense of belonging, wherever they work in our operations, and whatever their role. Our culture is grounded in our core values and our very strong commitment to delivering every day for our clients and their customers, getting everyone home safely, and caring about your wellbeing. We believe the diverse work we do really matters – whether we are connecting people and businesses to complex fibre networks, supporting large industrial projects and utilities, building modular spaces in which to learn, work or play, or delivering creative events to inspire and entertain. **Responsibilities:** * Adhere to and maintain compliance with all Health and Safety procedures within your work. * Engaging with our customers and clients, assisting them with all queries they have. * Daily and weekly job scheduling * Maintaining the Work Management System * Compiling daily operational reports * Preparation of PowerPoint slides * Engaging with service engineers providing them with support where required * Ad hoc administrative duties **Requirements:** * 1\+ years related experience * Excellent literacy, numeric and IT skills including proficiency in Microsoft Office * Knowledge of Microsoft Excel required * Excellent interpersonal and communication skills * Attention to detail * Experience working in a fast\-paced environment * Decisive, self\-motivated with the ability to represent the company in a professional manner at all times * Innovative, creative and proactive with a solution orientated approach **Benefits:** * Pension plan * Life assurance – 4 times your annual salary * Employee assistance programme * Refer a friend scheme * Maternity pay * Educational assistance * Career progression opportunities * Long Service Awards

title: Operational Risk Analyst
description: A global private markets investment firm is hiring a skilled and ambitious Operational Risk Associate to be based in their Dublin office. Reporting to the Head of Risk, you can expect strong exposure and autonomy in this role. Key Responsibilities: * Support the development and implementation of risk frameworks * Assist with control reviews and identify risk issues * Carry out end\-to\-end operational risk monitoring processes Requirements: * Experience in operational risk within financial services, ideally in asset management, investment banking, or private equity * A solid understanding of investment management and financial products If you’re interested in this opportunity, please apply. If you do not hear back within 72 hours, you can assume you have not been shortlisted on this occasion.

title: Overnight - Residence Experience Associate
description: **Role Summary:** We are seeking a motivated Overnight Resident Experience Associate to join our team at Glass Bottle, a premier Build\-to\-Rent (BTR) property located in Sandymount Strand, Co. Dublin. This is an ideal opportunity for someone looking to progress their career in the growing BTR industry, working for a company that is expanding its footprint in Ireland. The successful candidate, reporting to the Property Manager, will be integral to daily property operations, focusing on resident relations, health and safety, cleanliness and security. This highly visible role will involve regular interaction with residents and visitors helping ensure high standards in resident engagement and satisfaction. We’re seeking candidates with strong experience in customer engagement. This job would ideally suit candidates from hospitality roles like: Security Operative, Night Auditor, Assistant Night Manager / Night Manager. CompassRock has a dedicated and comprehensive training and certification program, and this role will have the potential to progress into more managerial role over time. Some of the training programs include The Property Institute’s Build to Rent course and Managing Safely Qualifications. **Company Overview:** CompassRock provides asset and property management services to the rental housing sector. Originally founded in the U.S., the company has expanded to the UK and is rapidly growing its portfolio of Build\-to\-Rent (BTR) assets across England and Europe. CompassRock is in an exciting growth phase, actively acquiring and developing new assets at a fast pace. With an entrepreneurial and innovative culture embedded in its DNA, the company values the dynamic, forward\-thinking individuals it employs. As CompassRock continues to expand, there will be abundant opportunities for career advancement and professional development. **Key Responsibilities:** **Customer Service \& Resident Relations:** * **Policy and Standards Adherence:** Uphold the company’s customer service standards to deliver high\-quality service and resident satisfaction. * **Issue Resolution:** Address resident complaints and actively troubleshoot problems to take ownership in challenging situations, escalating issues to the Property Manager when necessary. * **Resident Engagement Initiatives**: Support the planning and execution of community\-building activities that foster positive resident relations and engagement. * **Resident Support:** Provide residents with appropriate information throughout the tenancy lifespan, including providing renewal information, ensuring legal requirements are met and answering any questions that might occur during the tenancy lifespan. * **Relationship Building:** Cultivate strong relationships with residents, ensuring professional and prompt communication. Regularly engage with residents and prospective tenants to build strong rapport and trust. * **Leasing Support:** Provide administrative support to the Leasing team, as required. * **Enquiries**: Greet and assist prospective tenants and to qualify and register enquiries and to book viewing appointments at our properties (if required). * **Viewings:** Conduct informative and engaging viewings to prospective tenants being well\-equipped to answer any questions regarding the property (if required). * **Administrative Support:** Working with the leasing team to help maintain accurate and organised records of leases, applications, and tenant correspondence. **Operational Activities:** * **Property Support**: Contribute to maintaining a safe and attractive property for residents. Facilities Management Support: Assist the Property Manager with managing projects, overseeing outsourced services for building maintenance, and ensuring timely, high\-quality completion of tasks. * **Cleanliness**: Complete overnight cleaning of the amenity spaces, ensuring that the property is “resident ready” each day. * **‘Stock’ Coordination:** Collaborate with the property manager and procurement team to ensure adequate stock of goods and services for smooth property operations. Completing stock checks on a monthly basis. * **Inspections:** Conduct / support inventories, move\-in and move\-out inspections, documenting property conditions. * **Administrative Support:** Assist the Property Manager with all other general administrative duties, as required. **Targets and KPIs:** * Work diligently to meet specified targets and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) set by the management team. **Personal Qualities:** * **Attention to Detail:** Demonstrated focus on quality and compliance in previous work or education (desired). * **Proactive and Initiative\-Driven:** Able to foresee potential issues and address them before they escalate (desired). Sharing suggestions on how to improve efficiencies, encourage proactivity and support your colleagues. * **Flexibility:** Ability to adapt to changing circumstances, particularly in a fast\-paced property environment (desired). * **Positive, 'can\-do' Attitude:** A willingness to learn from, and provide support to, other team members, while delivering top levels of service to all residents (essential). **Skills:** * **Communication Skills:** Both written and verbal, for interacting with residents, staff, and external stakeholders (essential) * **Problem\-Solving:** Strong abilities in addressing resident disputes (essential). * **Customer Service:** Demonstrated experience in delivering exceptional customer service (essential). * **Computer Literacy:** While training on our property management systems will be provided, we require that the applicant be proficient with Microsoft Office and Outlook and have an aptitude for learning new software (essential). **Qualifications (desired):** * Bachelor’s degree in business administration, or a related field (desired) * Full clean Irish driver’s licence **Remuneration/ Bonuses / Benefits** * Base salary:€37,400 * Bonus: Discretionary Bonus Scheme * Benefits: Private health insurance; Pension Scheme; Cycle to Work Scheme; Company Sick Pay. **Working Hours:** * 5\-day working week (40 hours) including weekends (Saturday and Sunday). * This role includes nighttime shift work (between 22:30 – 07:30\) **Location:** This is a client facing role and must be based on site at the rostered location(primarily at Glass Bottle, Sandymount Strand, Dublin 4 but may be required to work at other locations as required by the business). **Equal Opportunities Statement:** We are an Equal Opportunities Employer, committed to creating an inclusive, diverse, and equitable workplace. We provide equal opportunities for everyone and offer reasonable adjustments to ensure all candidates have a fair chance during the recruitment process. ### **Company Overview** CompassRock provides asset and property management services to the rental housing sectors. Originally founded in the U.S., the company has expanded to the UK and is rapidly growing its portfolio of Build\-to\-Rent (BTR) assets across the UK and Ireland. Backed by private equity, CompassRock is in an exciting growth phase, actively acquiring and developing new assets at a fast pace. With an entrepreneurial and innovative culture embedded in its DNA, we value the dynamic, forward\-thinking individuals it employs.

title: Partner Solutions Architect, WWPS Global ISV Partners
description: **DESCRIPTION** --------------- Amazon Web Services (AWS) is seeking a Solutions Architect to join the Worldwide Public Sector ISV Partner Solutions Architecture team. In this role, you will serve as the embedded technical partner for strategic ISV partners in the security and intelligence domain whose development teams are headquartered in Dublin and the broader EMEA region. Your primary mission: help these partners build, modernize, and scale their solutions on AWS — driving architecture quality, accelerating product roadmap delivery, and enabling joint go\-to\-market motions that create value for public sector customers worldwide. This is a builder\-oriented role. You will work directly alongside partner engineering teams on architecture design, solution modernization, and hands\-on integration with AWS services. You will translate partner product roadmaps into AWS\-native architectures, identify opportunities for service adoption, and serve as the technical bridge between ISV R\&D organizations and AWS product/specialist teams. Ability to travel within EMEA (\~30%) for partner site visits and customer engagements. Must be a citizen of the EU with work authorization. Key job responsibilities * Serve as the primary AWS technical resource embedded with ISV partner development teams in Dublin/EMEA, providing architecture guidance, code\-level integration support, and solution design for security and intelligence workloads. * Conduct architecture reviews and Well\-Architected assessments on partner solutions, identifying optimization opportunities across cost, reliability, performance, and security pillars. * Drive solution modernization initiatives — helping partners containerize, adopt serverless patterns, integrate AI/ML capabilities (Amazon Bedrock, SageMaker), and leverage managed services to reduce undifferentiated heavy lifting. * Support sovereign cloud and restricted environment deployments (EU Sovereign) by providing architectural guidance on data residency, compliance frameworks, and regional service availability. * Enable partner go\-to\-market readiness including AWS Marketplace listing optimization, consulting partner onboarding, APN competency and program enrollment. A day in the life * You spend your morning in a design session with a partner engineering team, whiteboarding a migration from monolithic on\-prem DLP architecture to a containerized, event\-driven pattern on ECS/EKS with S3 and Lambda. * After lunch, you join a cross\-functional call with AWS storage specialists to resolve an FSx configuration for a partner workload that requires specific IOPS in a restricted compliance boundary. * You draft a section of a joint reference architecture document showing how a partner’s threat intelligence platform integrates with Amazon Bedrock for agentic AI workflows. * You check in with your counterpart PAM on upcoming customer meetings where the partner’s solution will be positioned, and prepare a technical deep\-dive slide for the session. * You close the day reviewing a partner’s Marketplace listing for architectural accuracy and completeness before their compliance certification submission. **BASIC QUALIFICATIONS** ------------------------ * Experience within specific technology domain areas (e.g. software development, cloud computing, systems engineering, infrastructure, security, networking, data \& analytics). * Experience in design, implementation, or consulting in applications and infrastructures * Experience in IT development or implementation/consulting in the software or Internet industries * Hands\-on experience with modern application architectures: containers (ECS/EKS/Docker), serverless (Lambda), event\-driven patterns, and microservices. * Familiarity with AI/ML services (Amazon Bedrock, SageMaker) and emerging patterns (RAG, agentic workflows, MCP integrations) **PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS** ---------------------------- * Experience working within software development or Internet\-related industries * Experience migrating or transforming legacy customer solutions to the cloud * Experience working with AWS technologies from a dev/ops perspective * Familiarity with AI/ML services (Amazon Bedrock, SageMaker) and emerging patterns (RAG, agentic workflows, MCP integrations). Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates. Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

title: Partnerships and Business Development Intern
description: **About Merces:** Merces is Ireland's first fitness rewards app \- and the first of its kind in the world. Our technology integrates with Strava to connect runs and walks to real rewards from the best cafés, wellness brands and local businesses. We launched in Dublin in June 2026 and we're growing fast. **The Role:** We're looking for a driven, commercially sharp and people\-focused Partnerships and Business Development Intern to join the Merces team this summer. This is not a sit\-at\-a\-desk\-and\-send\-emails internship. This is getting out there, building real relationships, and playing a genuine role in the growth of something that has never been done before. **What you'll be doing:** * Identifying and reaching out to run clubs across Dublin to bring them onto the Merces platform * Researching and approaching corporate sponsors in Dublin for our Impact Challenge fundraising programme * Building and managing a pipeline of partnership leads * Supporting the Founder and CEO in preparing for and following up on partnership meetings * Logging activity and tracking progress in our partnerships tracker * Getting involved in events and activations as they happen over the summer **What we're looking for:** * Someone who is naturally good with people and not afraid to pick up the phone or send a cold DM * Commercially minded with an interest in business development or sales * Organised, proactive and self\-directed \- you will own your pipeline * Based in Dublin for the summer * Ideally studying Business, Marketing or Commerce at Third Level * A genuine interest in fitness, wellness or the startup world is a big plus **Why this role is different:** The health, fitness and wellness industry is one of the fastest growing industries in the world right now. Getting hands\-on experience in partnerships and business development at a funded Irish startup at this stage is something genuinely worth having on your CV. You will be building real relationships with real businesses from day one. Not photocopying. Not making coffee. **What you get:** * Real experience building the commercial side of a fast\-moving Irish startup from the ground up * Direct access to and mentorship from the founder * Involvement in launch events and activations across Dublin and potentially London * Expenses covered * A summer that actually looks good on your CV **To apply:** Send us a DM on Instagram @club\_merces or email info@mercesapp.com with your CV and a quick note on why you'd be great for this role.

title: Pharmacy Healthcare Advisor - Allcare O'Reillys Coolock
description: We currently have an exciting opportunity for a **Pharmacy Healthcare Advisor** to join the teams in **Allcare Pharmacy Tonlegee Road** Allcare support is a company which specialises in supporting Community Pharmacies to deliver excellent personalised care to their community. The Allcare Pharmacy group is a network of pharmacies throughout Ireland which strive for customer service excellence where staff focus on their core skill of providing expert medical care, advice and value to their patients and customers. **Purpose Of Role** To deliver excellent customer service and to provide advice and information as required to customers on all product ranges available in the Pharmacy. To maintain the standard of category management and merchandising within the Pharmacy as directed by the Managing Pharmacist and in line with Company standards. **Duties And Responsibilities** * To provide product information and advice to customers across all product ranges in the Pharmacy, consulting with the Managing Pharmacist as appropriate. * To maintain good stock control including; rotation of stock, maintaining appropriate stock levels, using correct suppliers, and ensuring that the delivery details are correct as set down by the Managing Pharmacist. * To deliver professional and efficient customer service at all times, enhancing the relationship with Pharmacy Customers. * To work effectively as part of the Pharmacy team, being flexible and adaptable, assisting in all areas of the Pharmacy operation as required by the Managing Pharmacist. * To maintain cooperative and professional working relationships with colleagues at all times. * To fulfil the request for specific over the counter medicines appropriately using WWHAM questioning and recommend a suitable medicine referring to the Pharmacist where appropriate. * To be vigilant and adhere to the correct procedures with regards to security and the handling of cash and receipts within the Pharmacy. * To ensure that the Pharmacy is kept clean and tidy and any Health and Safety issues are brought promptly to the attention of the Pharmacy Manager. * To participate in training and development programmes provided by the Pharmacy and to be proactive in developing the job function. * Any other level of pro\-activity and an ability to work on own initiative. * Build relationships with others in the pharmacy to create a positive working environment. * Participate in staff training programmes and implementation and be proactive in developing the role function. * To work effectively as part of the Pharmacy team, being flexible and adaptable assisting in all areas of the pharmacy operation. **Customer Focused** * We are passionate about exceeding expectations: * making our community pharmacies the local destination of choice * Delivering customer service to the highest quality * To provide advice and information as required to customers on all product ranges available in the Pharmacy. * To deliver professional and efficient customer service at all times, enhancing the relationship with Pharmacy Customers. **Qualifications, Experience \& Skills Required** * 1 years experience in Pharmacy/Retail/Hospitality is preferable * Cashier experience an advantage * Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal * Ability to use discretion when addressing customer queries and to treat customer/patient information confidentially * Good attention to detail is essential * Organised with a proven ability to multi\-task successfully under pressure * Ability to work on own initiative and as part of a team * Basic IT skills are an advantage *\#allcare*

title: Planner / Scheduler - Dublin
description: **Join STS Group as our next Planner in Dublin** Part of the Dussman Group of companies, STS Group is a well\-established electrical and instrumentation engineering contractor with mission critical projects across Europe and the Middle East. We have lots of work ongoing and a full\-order book ahead, we need more good people to join our team. **The Opportunity** We are looking for an experienced **Planner / Scheduler** to join our team for a data centre project in Dublin. The successful applicant will be responsible for planning activities and progress reporting of the electrical packages of work. **Here are some of the key duties of this role:** * Engineering / construction / commissioning schedule development \& maintenance, ensuring accuracy and timely updating of construction schedule. * Complete schedule health checks prior to baselining for accuracy, integrity and critical path validation. * Resource loading schedules, producing and tracking applicable KPIs. * Monitoring budgets for scope growth/changes * Track planned versus actual EV progress. * Working with team members to ensure systems in place to capture and log any issues via the appropriate change control mechanisms * Attending site meetings as required (internal and client based) * Productivity tracking of trades/craft labour Spent vs Earned hours **Key Qualifications Required:** * 2\+ years' experience in planner/scheduler roles * Excellent communication skills in the English language, with detailed and accurate report writing * Proven experience using project planning software (e.g: Primevera, MS Project) **Desirable but not Essential** * Electrical trade or tertiary\-level electrical (or mechanical) engineering qualification. * Mission Critical MEP projects experience \- data centres or pharmaceutical plants **Why Join The STS Group?** * A dynamic, growing company in the construction industry. * A supportive team where your skills will be valued and developed. * Competitive remuneration package. ***We are an Equal Opportunities Employer, we encourage people from all walks of life to apply.***

title: Planning Graduate - 2026 Suir Engineering Graduate
description: We have an exciting opportunity for Planning Graduate to join our 18\-month graduate programme. Here in Suir Engineering we are a leading international provider of innovative Mechanical, Electrical and Instrumentation Engineering Solutions. Our core sectors are Energy, Power and Renewables, Pharma, Food \& Beverage, and Data Centres. We provide best in class contracting solutions with in\-house, flexible resources capable of delivering a personable and consistent service to our customers. Over the past 40 years we have delivered projects for Irish and multinational clients across the globe. We are currently expanding significantly and are delivering large scale projects across Ireland, the U.K, Germany, Denmark \& Sweden. **We Have Opportunities To Join Our Programme In** * Dublin Office * Waterford Office Travel will be required various sites as requested. **Our Graduate Programme** In addition to on the job experience we also provide some specialized programmes for our Graduate Trainees * Soft Skills Modules – communications / presentation skills * Career Pathway Conversations – learn how different parts of our business work * Group Projects \& Presentations * Site Visits **Is this Graduate Program for me?** * Level 8 or relevant bachelor’s degree in relevant field * Analytical and problem\-solving skills with attention to detail. * A proactive attitude with a willingness to learn and take on challenges. * Able to work in a team environment * Pride in the quality and presentation of work * Flexibility to work on different sites and relocate if required. **What will I be doing?** * Continuously model the steps of the construction project in different diagrams. Monitor and report on project progress. * Make decisions related to logistics and resources. * Monitor \& Report on Internal productivity and issue Earned Value Analysis on a weekly basis. * Attend meetings with the Project Team to discuss progress made and any issues that arise over the course of the project. * Any other reasonable and relevant duties as requested by your manager, necessary to meet the ongoing needs of the company. * Daily \& weekly programme updates with the client. **In Return From Suir Engineering** * Salary starting from €40,000 per year * 23 days annual leave * Company Pension contributions \- no waiting periods * Paid Maternity and Paternity Leave * Private Health Insurance contribution * Life and Disability cover * EAP for employees and their families * Educational Assistance, CPD, Chartership supports and upskilling opportunities * Opportunities for international assignments to work on one of our European projects in Sweden, Denmark, or Germany. Our way of working is called the Suir Way, designed to help us on a journey of continuous improvement. * Our people are empowered to make changes to ensure a quality install through effective ways of working. * Our people have a voice and feel empowered. * We have an in\-built culture of innovation, learning and continuous improvement. * Value is delivered every step of the journey. Our efficient processes deliver value. * We have a standard, consistent and fully integrated health, safety, environment, and quality system within the business. Suir Engineering are an equal opportunities employer; we value our greatest asset …. our People. We do not require the support of Recruitment agencies at this time.

title: Portfolio Operations Associate
description: **Overview** Fisher Investments Ireland is the local entity of Fisher Investments, a privately held investment management firm that provides professional discretionary portfolio management services for high net worth individuals and some of the world's preeminent institutions. Fisher Investments' presence in Europe has grown significantly over the past decade, with a recent expansion into Ireland and Luxembourg. We're looking for someone with an analytical background to join our team as a Portfolio Operations Associate based in our Dublin office, and be a part of helping shape the future of Portfolio Operations at Fisher. **The Opportunity:** As a Portfolio Operations Associate at Fisher Investments Ireland, you will be part of Fisher's Portfolio Management Group. You will maintain investment strategies for our international retail accounts. Your primary responsibilities will involve generating account\-specific trade orders; helping coordinate and execute portfolio shifts, regularly working with Portfolio Operations Analysts, Portfolio Engineers and Traders; matching trades with counterparties; and processing foreign currency conversions. As a member of this newer team you will be encouraged to suggest strategic solutions to automate and scale our daily processes, increasing our efficiency while providing unparalleled service to our valued clients. **The Day\-to\-Day:** * Oversee trade order generation in new client accounts and existing client accounts to align with the appropriate strategy * Produce account level trade orders to implement strategy level changes * Facilitate prompt and accurate execution of client trade requests * Support client services by serving as a point of contact for questions related to individual client portfolios * Perform operational tasks related to client portfolio management * Help design, execute, and maintain scalable workflows and systems that support the future growth of our business **Your Qualifications:** * A university degree or equivalent combination of education and experience * No professional experience required, while some is favoured * Be able to follow operational workflows * A sound knowledge of financial concepts, markets, and current events * Experience implementing process improvements * Native or fluent English language skills. Proficiency in other European languages is beneficial but not required * Eagerness to help expand a growing team * Experience communicating on a team and able to work to a high level of detail **Why Fisher Investments Ireland:** The global Fisher organisation distinguishes itself by putting clients first, providing unmatched service, and taking a personalised approach to investing. You can feel confident knowing that Fisher Investments and Fisher Investments Ireland align with our clients’ priorities by using a simple and transparent fee structure and recognised European custodians. It’s the people that make the Fisher purpose possible, and to help our employees meet their long\-term goals, we offer an array of benefits, including: * 100% paid premiums for our top\-tier supplemental healthcare and dental plans for employees and their qualified dependents * 28 days annual leave, with the ability to purchase up to 3 additional days per year, plus up to 11 paid holidays * Enhanced maternity pay package with 26 weeks’ top up to full base pay for eligible employees * $10,000\* fertility, hormonal health and family\-forming benefit * A retirement pension plan, featuring a 9% company pension contribution of your base pay and an additional company match of up to 5% of base pay * Monthly gym subsidy of up to €50 per month * Employee Assistance Program and other emotional wellbeing services * A collaborative working environment that practices ongoing training, educational support and employee appreciation events * This is an in\-office role. Based on your role, tenure, and performance eligibility you may have the opportunity to participate in our hybrid work from home program. This program is subject to change. * Employees residing outside of the US will be eligible for the $10,000 equivalent in their local currency. FISHER INVESTMENTS IRELAND IS AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER

title: Pre-TenderingCoordinator (Civil Engineering)
description: **Role: Pre\-Tendering Coordinator (Civil Engineering)** **Country: Republic of Ireland** **Location: Sallins, Co. Kildare** **About Wills Bros Ltd** Wills Bros is seeking an experienced a Pre\-Tendering Coordinator (Civil Engineering) to join our Precontracts team at our Sallins office. This is a unique opportunity to play a key role in a live, high\-security environment, contributing to the successful delivery of complex structural and civil works while upholding the highest standards of quality, safety, and compliance. This is a full\-time, office\-based role in our Sallins office. **Role Overview** Under the oversight of the Lead Prequalification Manager, the Assistant Prequalification Manager will lead the preparation of select Pre‑Qualification Questionnaires (PQQs), Expressions of Interest (EOIs) and selection stage submissions for public and private sector civil engineering projects. The role involves close collaboration with Engineering, Commercial, Quality, HSEQ, Technical Bids and Site teams and requires strong technical writing, organisation and commercial awareness. **Key Responsibilities** * Lead and coordinate PQQ, EOI and selection stage submissions, managing inputs, timelines and reviews * Interpret procurement documentation and scoring methodologies to ensure full compliance * Draft clear, evidence\-based responses that reflect Wills Bros’ experience, capability and differentiators * Manage and maintain ISO, HSEQ and sustainability content, ensuring it is current and aligned to client requirements * Maintain project experience records, CVs and evidence libraries to support repeatable, high\-quality submissions * Coordinate inputs from project, operational and functional teams to meet deadlines and quality standards * Manage procurement portals and tender databases, tracking opportunities and submission status * Support and mentor junior staff involved in prequalification submissions **Qualifications \& Experience** * Relevant third level qualification in Civil Engineering, Construction Management, Project Management, Quality, Health \& Safety, Business, Marketing or a related discipline * Construction/civil engineering experience within the Irish and/or EU market * Demonstrable experience in prequalification, pre‑contracts, bidding or tendering * Excellent document production skills, with the ability to simplify complex information and present it clearly * Strong written communication, organisation and attention to detail * Working knowledge of Irish public procurement and tender processes **What We Offer** * 24 days’ annual leave plus bank holidays. * Employee Assistance Programme. * Death in Service benefit (4x basic salary). * Pension scheme. * Income Protection. * Digital Doctor. * Bike to Work scheme. * Company\-funded professional development, including courses, certifications, and memberships in accredited bodies such as Engineers Ireland. * Ongoing professional development and career progression opportunities. * A supportive, collaborative working environment. * The chance to contribute to high\-profile, impactful projects. If you are a motivated Pre\-Tendering Coordinator looking to contribute to a high\-profile infrastructure project and grow your career with a leading tier 1 main contractor, we would be delighted to hear from you. Please note: We are not accepting applications from recruitment agencies at this time. **How to Apply:** Apply now or contact Paula Murphy for a confidential discussion. Alternatively, email your CV to hr@willsbros.com Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Application question(s): * Do you have experience in Civil Engineering/Construction Industry in a pre\-Contracts/Bid/technical role? title: Pricing Analyst
description: Barden is delighted to have been engaged by our client to identify an Pricing Analyst to join their team on a full\-time permanent basis. The hiring manager is seeking a commercially minded finance professional with expertise in financial modelling, bid support, and stakeholder partnering, capable of delivering accurate, data\-driven commercial solutions. **ABOUT THE ROLE:** * Develop detailed cost models and pricing solutions for tenders and bids ensuring commercial viability and alignment with client requirements. * Partner with Sales, Operations, Procurement, and Finance teams to translate service specifications into deliverable and cost\-effective solutions. * Perform financial modelling, sensitivity analysis, and scenario planning to assess key assumptions, risks, and pricing strategies. * Support the preparation of business cases, commercial submissions, and bid responses, often working to challenging deadlines. * Validate cost assumptions and delivery models to ensure accurate, competitive, and robust pricing proposals. * Drive continuous improvement of pricing methodologies, tools, and processes while monitoring market trends and industry best practices. **ABOUT THE PERSON:** * Experienced commercial finance professional with a background in pricing, bid support, and cost modelling. * Strong track record of supporting complex tenders. * Skilled in building detailed cost models. * Advanced Excel user with expertise in financial modelling and pricing analysis. * Effective collaborator, partnering with operational stakeholders to design practical and cost\-effective delivery models. * Commercially focused, with the ability to balance customer value, profitability, and risk.

title: Procurement Buyer
description: LetsGetChecked is a global healthcare solutions company that provides the tools to manage health from home through health testing, virtual care, genetic sequencing, and medication delivery for a wide range of health and wellness conditions. LetsGetChecked's end\-to\-end model includes manufacturing, logistics, lab analysis, physician support, and prescription fulfillment. Founded in 2015 and co\-headquartered in Dublin and Atlanta, LetsGetChecked empowers people to take control of their health and live longer, happier lives. **Procurement Buyer** As a Procurement Buyer, you will be a cornerstone of the Supply Chain team, driving both operational excellence and strategic growth. You will manage the direct materials supply base to ensure our manufacturing and lab operations never miss a beat. This role is perfect for a data\-driven professional who thrives in an SAP S/4HANA environment and is passionate about Continuous Improvement (CI). **What You'll Do** * Manage purchase order placement, tracking, and delivery in strict alignment with site planning and inventory requirements. * Provide accurate, forward\-looking volume outlooks to supplier partners to facilitate robust medium and long\-term capacity planning. * Act as the primary point of contact for order status and scheduling, collaborating closely with Logistics Specialists to resolve transit or customs issues. * Proactively track open orders to identify potential shortages, highlight delivery risks, and escalate critical supply issues to leadership when necessary. * Partner with Planning, NPI (New Product Introduction), Logistics, and Quality teams to troubleshoot and resolve supply constraints. * Secure optimal pricing and lead times while continuously auditing supplier performance to ensure cost\-competitiveness and risk reduction. * Support the implementation of Sustainability \& Responsibility (S\&R) and supplier diversity commitments within sourcing strategies and contract requirements. * Collaborate with the Quality team to ensure the Approved Vendor List (ASL) and ERP Master Data remain current for all assigned consumables * Achieve all set Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) and targets related to cost savings, delivery precision, and inventory turns. **Who You Are** * A Third Level Qualification in a relevant field (Technical, Manufacturing, or Business) or equivalent relevant experience. * 2\+ years of relevant experience, demonstrating a clear understanding of budgeting/forecasting and the effects of cost savings. * SAP or a similar ERP system, with proficiency in logistics and procurement modules for shipment tracking, inventory control, and order management. * Advanced Excel (Pivot Tables, VLOOKUPs) * Excellent communication, negotiation, and problem\-solving skills. * Experience in negotiating with carriers, freight forwarders, and suppliers to secure cost\-effective terms and competitive freight rates. * Experience in negotiating, and problem\-solving skills. The ideal candidate will also demonstrate a proactive attitude towards upskilling, and a genuine desire to expand professionally. **Benefits** Alongside a base salary of **€42,500 \- €45,000** we offer a range of benefits including: * Health insurance and an Employee Assistance Programme * Pension matching contribution * Annual Compensation Reviews * LetsGetChecked has a flexible annual leave policy * Free monthly LetsGetChecked tests as we are not only focused on the well being of our patients but also the well being of our teams * A referral bonus programme to reward you for helping us hire the best talent * Internal Opportunities and Careers Clinics to help you progress your career within the company. * Bike to work scheme \- and secure bike storage onsite. * Maternity, Paternity, Parental and Wedding leave. **Why LetsGetChecked** At LetsGetChecked, we are revolutionizing healthcare by making it more accessible, convenient, and personalized. Our mission is to empower individuals with the knowledge and tools they need to manage their health proactively, so they can live longer, happier lives. By joining our team, you will be part of a dynamic and innovative company that is dedicated to improving lives through cutting\-edge technology and compassionate care. We value our employees and invest in their growth, offering opportunities for professional development and career advancement. Together, we can make a meaningful impact on the future of healthcare and help people take control of their health journey. Join us in our commitment to transforming healthcare for the better. **Our Commitment to Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion** At LetsGetChecked, we are committed to fostering an inclusive environment that celebrates diversity in all its forms. We believe that the diversity of thought, background, and experience strengthens our teams and drives innovation. We are an equal\-opportunity employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, ethnicity, religion, color, place of birth, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, military service status, or disability status. Our goal is to ensure that everyone feels valued and empowered to thrive. Please review our Candidate Privacy Policy. To learn more about LetsGetChecked and our mission to help people live longer, healthier lives please visit https://www.letsgetchecked.com/careers/

title: Product Owner
description: **About us** At Davy, it’s the unique talents of all our people that have been the foundations of our success for 100 years. As we continue to grow, so do you. Because you are not just part of our team – you are a key player in shaping our future. At Davy, you are the difference. Established in 1926, the Davy Group sits at the heart of wealth and capital in Ireland, providing a broad range of services to high\-net\-worth individuals, self\-directed investors, small\-to\-medium enterprises, credit unions, corporations, and domestic \& international institutional investors. Our culture is shaped around three values, which are at the heart of everything we do: client success, one Davy, and building a proud legacy. Sharing the same goals, we work together to deliver exceptional outcomes for our clients. As part of a team of over 900, you will be encouraged to learn and to grow, both professionally and personally. **About the role** An exciting opportunity has opened up for a Product Owner to join our team at Davy. In the role, the successful candidate will: * Define and communicate the product vision for the squad, aligning to business strategy * Own and manage the backlog, prioritised against value outcomes and stakeholder requirements, and lead refinement, sprint planning and review sessions for their squad * Translate business requirements into features and detailed user stories with acceptance criteria * Drive roadmap planning across release horizons and quarterly planning cycles * Act as primary liaison between business and technology, and wider stakeholders as required, including reporting and wider updates * Execute with Agile ways of working and methodologies * Manage the governance and risk agenda for the squad appropriately, adhering to compliance and regulatory standards, participating in governance forums as required and maintain an appropriate audit trail **Requirements** **About you** We are looking for somebody who has the following background and skills: Experience \& Background * 5\+ experience as a Product Owner / Product Manager in technology\-driven environments * Strong track record delivering digital products and transformation initiatives across industries (e.g., financial services, eCommerce, or similar) * Experience working across global, multi\-stakeholder environments with distributed teams * Background in end\-to\-end product delivery, from concept through to launch and optimisation * Previous experience as a Business Analyst or similar role is highly beneficial Product Ownership \& Delivery * Demonstrated ability to define and execute product strategy and roadmap aligned to business goals * Expertise in managing and prioritising a product backlog, ensuring clear, actionable user stories and acceptance criteria * title: Proposals Engineer

title: Quality Control System Specialist

The successful candidate will be responsible for supporting QC laboratory systems and analytical instrumentation through maintenance, troubleshooting, lifecycle management, software administration, compliance activities, and continuous improvement initiatives. This role offers the opportunity to work within a highly regulated GMP environment supporting laboratory operations and quality objectives. **KEY RESPONSIBIEITIES** * Perform planned and unplanned maintenance of QC laboratory instruments and systems. * Support installation, qualification, operation, troubleshooting, and retirement of analytical instments. Ensure laboratory systems remain compliant with data integrity, regulatory, and industry requirements. * Manage and support software applications including Empower, LabX, Signals, and related laboratory systems.Act as a Super User for QC systems and support method requests where required. * Provide technical troubleshooting support and participate in investigations, deviations, and root cause analysis activities. * Manage and support Change Controls, CAPAs, Deviations, and system\-related quality events. * Author, review, and revise SOPs, work instructions, and controlled documentation. * Support regulatory inspections, internal audits, and compliance assessments as a Subject Matter Expert (SME). * Drive continuous improvement initiatives to enhance QC laboratory efficiency, reliability, and compliance. * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to ensure laboratory objectives and business priorities are achieved. **REQUIRED EXPERIENCE \& SKILLS** **Technical Skills** * Strong experience supporting analytical laboratory instrumentation within GMP\-regulated environments. * Experience with computerized systems validation (CSV) is highly desirable. * Hands\-on experience with Empower software is preferred. * Familiarity with laboratory software platforms such as LabX, Signals, or equivalent systems. * Strong understanding of Data Integrity requirements and GMP compliance. * Experience managing change controls, deviations, CAPAs, and quality system documentation. * Ability to troubleshoot complex laboratory system and instrumentation issues. * Strong analytical, organizational, and problem\-solving skills. **Business \& Communication Skills** * Ability to manage multiple priorities and meet critical deadlines. * Strong communication and stakeholder management skills. * Ability to work independently and collaboratively within cross\-functional teams. * Flexible and adaptable in a dynamic manufacturing environment. **QUALIFICATIONS** * Third\-level qualification in Science, Engineering, Biotechnology, Chemistry, Pharmaceutical Science, or a related discipline. This is an excellent opportunity for a laboratory systems professional seeking to join a highly regulated biotechnology manufacturing environment with long\-term contract potential.
